messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Access Control Lists (ACLs)?

Access Control Lists (ACLs): About Access Control Lists (ACLs) Access Control Lists (ACLs) let a network administrator permit or deny passage of traffic based on network addresses, protocols, service ports, and other packet attributes. ACLs are composed of one or more Access Control Entries (called ACEs). Each ACE defines a filter criteria and an action, either permit or deny. If the traffic matches the filter criteria, the specified action is taken. The permit action permits the traffic to continue through the switch. The deny action causes the traffic to be discarded (dropped). ACEs can also log or count matching traffic. Three ACL types are supported; IPv4, IPv6, and MAC. Each ACL type is focused on relevant frame or packet characteristics. ACLs must be applied (using an apply access-list command) to take effect. ACLs can be applied to interfaces (including LAGs), VLANs, or the Control Plane. Access Control Entries (ACEs) are listed according to priority by sequence number and processed in lowest to highest sequence number order.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about About applying ACLs to VLANs?

About applying ACLs to VLANs: ACLs can be applied to VLANs only in the inbound (ingress) direction. Sequence numbering If no sequence number is specified, the software appends new ACEs to the end of the ACL with a sequence number equal to the highest ACE currently in the list plus 10. The sequence numbers may be resequenced using the access-list resequence command.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list copy command do?
The access-list copy command copies an IPv4, IPv6, or MAC ACL to a new destination ACL or overwrites an existing ACL. Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-ACL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list copy command?
The syntax of the access-list copy command is: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-ACL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list resequence command do?
The access-list resequence command resequences the ACE sequence numbers in an ACL. Syntax: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list resequence command?
The syntax of the access-list resequence command is: access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list reset command?
The syntax of the access-list reset command is: access-list {all|ip <ACL-NAME>|IPv6 <ACL-NAME>|mac <ACL-NAME>} reset.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list control-plane command do?

The apply access-list control-plane command applies an ACL to the specified VRF. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the specified VRF. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list control-plane command?
The syntax of the apply access-list control-plane command is: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command applies an ACL to the interface (Individual front plane port) or Link Aggregation Group (LAG) identified by the current interface or LAG context. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the current interface or LAG identified by the current interface or LAG context. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> {in|out}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command?
The syntax of the apply access-list (to interface or LAG) command is: apply access-list {ip|IPv6|mac} <ACL-NAME> {in|out}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command do?

The apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command applies an ACL to the interface VLAN (or range of interface VLANs) identified by the current interface VLAN context. Using the apply access-list command on an interface VLAN interface with an already-applied ACL of the same direction and type will replace the currently-applied ACL. The no form of this command removes application of the ACL from the interface VLAN (or range of interface VLANs) identified by the current interface VLAN context. Syntax: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> {routed-in|routed-out}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command?
The syntax of the apply access-list (to interface VLAN) command is: apply access-list {ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME> {routed-in|routed-out}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command do?

The clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command clears the hit counts for ACLs applied to the specified Control Plane VRF. Reorders the sequence numbers in an address object group. Syntax: clear access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command?
The syntax of the clear access-list hitcounts control-plane command is: clear access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the object-group {ip|IPv6} address <OBJECT-GROUP-NAME> reset command?
The syntax of the object-group {ip|IPv6} address <OBJECT-GROUP-NAME> reset command is: object-group {ip|IPv6} address <OBJECT-GROUP-NAME> reset.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show access-list control-plane command do?

The show access-list control-plane command shows information about your defined ACLs that have been applied to the Control Plane. When show access-list control-plane is entered without parameters, information for all ACLs applied to the Control Plane is shown. The parameters filter the list of ACLs for which information is shown. Available filtering includes: • The content of a specific ACL that has been applied to the Control Plane. • All ACLs of a specific type that have been applied to the Control Plane. • All ACLs applied to the Control Plane for a specific VRF. Syntax: show access-list [ip|IPv6] [<ACL-NAME>] control-plane [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show access-list control-plane command?
The syntax of the show access-list control-plane command is: show access-list [ip|IPv6] [<ACL-NAME>] control-plane [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command do?

The show access-list hitcounts control-plane command shows the hit count of the number of times an ACL (applied to the Control Plane) has matched a packet for ACEs with the count keyword. For ACEs without the count keyword, a dash is shown in place of a hit count. Syntax: show access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME> [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command?
The syntax of the show access-list hitcounts control-plane command is: show access-list hitcounts [{ip|IPv6} <ACL-NAME>] control-plane vrf <VRF-NAME> [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities command do?
The show capacities command shows system capacities and their values for all features or a specific feature. Syntax: show capacities <FEATURE> [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities command?
The syntax of the show capacities command is: show capacities <FEATURE> [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities-status command do?
The show capacities-status command shows system capacities status and their values for all features or a specific feature. Syntax: show capacities-status <FEATURE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities-status command?
The syntax of the show capacities-status command is: show capacities-status <FEATURE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Classifier policies?

Classifier policies: overview Classifier policies let a network administrator define sets of rules based on network traffic addressing or other header content, and use these rules to restrict or alter the passage of traffic through the switch. Choosing the rule criteria is called Classification, and one such rule, or list, is called a policy. Classification is achieved by creating a traffic class. The three types of classes (MAC, IPv4, and IPv6) are each focused on relevant frame/packet characteristics. Classes can be configured to match or ignore almost any frame or packet header field. Network traffic passing through a switch can be classified based on many different frame/packet characteristics including, but not limited to: •.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply policy command do?

The apply policy command applies a policy to the global config context. Only one policy can be globally applied at a time. Applying a policy globally again, replaces the previous globally applied policy. The no form of this command removes application of the global policy. | 86 | | AOS-CX 10.06 ACLs and Classifier Policies Guide. Syntax: apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply policy command?
The syntax of the apply policy command is: apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the class copy command do?

The class copy command copies a IPv4, IPv6, or MAC class to a new destination class or overwrites an existing class. Copying a class copies all entries as well. Syntax: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-CLASS>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class copy command?
The syntax of the class copy command is: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-CLASS>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the class resequence command do?
The class resequence command resequence numbering in an IPv4, IPv6, or MAC class. Syntax: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class resequence command?
The syntax of the class resequence command is: class {ip|IPv6|mac} <CLASS-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQUENCE-NUMBER> <INCREMENT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the class reset command?
The syntax of the class reset command is: class { all | ip <CLASS-NAME> | IPv6 <CLASS-NAME> |mac <CLASS-NAME> } reset.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy command do?

The policy command creates or modifies classifier policy and policy entries. A policy is made up of one or more policy entries ordered and prioritized by sequence numbers. Each entry has an IPv4/IPv6/MAC class and zero or more policy actions associated with it. A policy must be applied using the apply command. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy command?
The syntax of the policy command is: policy <POLICY-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy copy command do?

The policy copy command copies a policy to a new destination policy or overwrites an existing policy. Copying a policy copies all its entries as well. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-POLICY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy copy command?
The syntax of the policy copy command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> copy <DESTINATION-POLICY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy resequence command do?
The policy resequence command resequences numbering in a policy. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy resequence command?
The syntax of the policy resequence command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> resequence <STARTING-SEQ-NUM> <INCREMENT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the policy reset command do?

The policy reset command changes the user-specified policy configuration to match the active policy configuration. Use this command when a discrepancy exists between what the user configured and what is active and accepted by the system. Syntax: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the policy reset command?
The syntax of the policy reset command is: policy <POLICY-NAME> reset.

section: Ingress Routed VLAN Policy with IPv6 classes

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Ingress Routed VLAN Policy with IPv6 classes?

Ingress Routed VLAN Policy with IPv6 classes: Ingress IPv4 and/or IPv6 Analytics Data Collection (ADC) 8320 and 8325: There are two lookups available on Egress for these features. Applying both IPv4 and IPv6 ACLs on Routed-Egress at the same time is not permitted. Egress features that use one lookup:

topic: Ingress Routed VLAN Policy with IPv6 classes

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Egress Routed VLAN IPv6 ACL?

Egress Routed VLAN IPv6 ACL: 8325 TCAM lookup sharing: TCAM lookups for the following features are shared between corresponding IPv4 and IPv6 features. For example, if three IPv4 features are used with three equivalent IPv6 features, only three TCAM lookups are consumed instead of six. This is assuming the total number of IPv4 and IPv6 entries in each feature are less than 768. Ingress Port IPv4 ACL; Ingress Port IPv6 ACL Ingress VLAN IPv4 ACL; Ingress VLAN IPv6 ACL Ingress Routed Port Policy with IPv4 classes; Ingress Routed Port Policy with IPv6 classes Ingress Routed VLAN Policy with IPv4 classes; Ingress Routed VLAN Policy with IPv6 classes Ingress IPv4 Analytics Data Collection (ADC); Ingress IPv6 Analytics Data Collection (ADC)
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Ingress Routed VLAN IPv4 ACL?

Ingress Routed VLAN IPv4 ACL: Ingress VLAN IPv4 ACL Ingress Port MAC ACL Ingress VLAN MAC ACL Ingress IPv6 Analytics Data Collection (ADC) Ingress IPv4 Analytics Data Collection (ADC) Ingress Port Policy with IPv6 classes Ingress Port Policy with IPv4 and/or MAC classes Ingress VLAN Policy with IPv6 classes Ingress VLAN Policy with IPv4 and/or MAC classes Ingress Global Policy with IPv6 classes Ingress Global Policy with IPv4 and/or MAC classes IPv6 Control Plane Policing IPv4 Control Plane Policing MAC Control Plane Policing Ingress Control Plane Policing Ingress Routed Port IPv6 Policy Ingress Routed Port IPv4 Policy Ingress Routed VLAN IPv6 Policy Ingress Routed VLAN IPv4 Policy Ingress L3 Statistics Ingress IPv6 VSX Ingress IPv4 VSX Ingress ARP VSX Ingress MAC VSX Egress Routed Port IPv4 ACL Egress Routed VLAN IPv6 ACL Egress VLAN IPv6 ACL Egress Routed VLAN IPv4 ACL Egress VLAN IPv4 ACL Egress Control Plane Policing Egress L3 Statistics L4 port ranges Any ACE or class entry that uses 'lt', 'gt', 'range', or port groups will attempt to use dedicated hardware resources called L4 port ranges.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the access-list mac <ACL-NAME> command do?
The access-list mac <ACL-NAME> command is documented in this guide. Syntax: no access-list mac <ACL-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the access-list mac <ACL-NAME> command?
The syntax of the access-list mac <ACL-NAME> command is: no access-list mac <ACL-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip local-proxy-arp command do?

The ip local-proxy-arp command enables local proxy ARP on the specified interface. Local proxy ARP is supported on Layer 3 physical interfaces and on VLAN interfaces. To enable local proxy ARP on an interface, routing must be enabled on that interface. The no form of this command disables local proxy ARP on the specified interface. Syntax: no ip local-proxy-arp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip local-proxy-arp command?
The syntax of the ip local-proxy-arp command is: no ip local-proxy-arp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR> command do?

The IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR> command specifies a permanent static neighbor entry in the ARP table (for IPv6 neighbors). The no form of this command deletes a permanent static neighbor entry from the ARP table. Syntax: no IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR> command is: no IPv6 neighbor <IPv6-ADDR> mac <MAC-ADDR>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd command do?

The bfd command enables BFD support on the switch. BFD is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables BFD and removes all related configuration settings. To disable BFD, but retain configuration settings, use the command bfd disable. Syntax: no bfd.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd command?
The syntax of the bfd command is: no bfd.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command enables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. BFD is asynchronous and echo mode is supported. The no form of this command disables BFD under VRRP for the specified IP address. Syntax: no bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the bfd <IPv4-ADDR> command is: no bfd <IPv4-ADDR>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf bfd command do?

The ip ospf bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv2 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option. Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd command?
The syntax of the ip ospf bfd command is: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command enables BFD for OSPFv3 on the current interface. The interface must have OSPFv3 enabled on it. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. The no form of this command sets the current interface to the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Disables BFD on the current OSPFv3 interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Enables BGP to register with BFD to receive fast peering session deactivation messages from BFD. The no form of this command disables BGP for BFD. NOTE: Currently, BFD is supported only with IPv4 neighbors. Support to enable BFD with IPv6 neighbors will be added in a future release. Shows information for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd command is: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor <IP-ADDR> activate command do?

The neighbor <IP-ADDR> activate command this command enables the address-family capability and exchange of information specific to an address family with a BGP neighbor. The no form of this command removes the address-family capability and disables the exchange of routes for the specified address-family with the BGP neighbor. Sets the advertisement interval, which defines the length of time between transmission of BGP routing updates. 192 AOS-CX 10.06 Command-Line Interface Guide The no form of this command restores the default value. Default values are 30 seconds for external BGP peer and 5 seconds for internal BGP peer. Enables additional path capability for BGP as described in RFC 7911. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} advertisement-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor <IP-ADDR> activate command?
The syntax of the neighbor <IP-ADDR> activate command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} advertisement-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> next-hop-unchanged command do?

The neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> next-hop-unchanged command enables the neighbor to preserve next-hop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. The no form of this command resets the peer next-hop-unchanged status to default. Syntax: no neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> next-hop-unchanged.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> next-hop-unchanged command?
The syntax of the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> next-hop-unchanged command is: no neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> next-hop-unchanged.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> orf-prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> in command do?

The neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> orf-prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> in command applies an inbound prefix list filter to filter the distribution of BGP neighbor information. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not applying the prefix list filter. NOTE: This command must be used only along with the ORF capability to take effect. Configures a specific neighbor, or all neighbors in a peer-group, as passive, which means that they will not initiate the TCP session. The neighbor connection must be reset using clear ip bgp for this setting to take effect. The no form of this command enables the neighbor to initiate the TCP session. Enables message digest5 (MD5) authentication on a TCP connection between two BGP neighbors. When the password is applied to a peer-group, all the neighbors that are part of peer-group inherit the configured setting. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} passive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> orf-prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> in command?
The syntax of the neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> orf-prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> in command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} passive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no shutdown command do?
The no shutdown command is documented in this guide. Syntax: no shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no shutdown command?
The syntax of the no shutdown command is: no shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint post-configuration command do?

The checkpoint post-configuration command enables creation of system generated checkpoints when configuration changes occur. This feature is enabled by default. The no form of this command disables system generated checkpoints. Syntax: no checkpoint post-configuration.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration command?
The syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration command is: no checkpoint post-configuration.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in command do?

The apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in command is documented in this guide. Syntax: no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in; Context: config-vlan: ; apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in; no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in.

syntax:
- no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in
- apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in
- no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in command?
The syntax of the apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in command is: no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in; Context: config-vlan: ; apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in; no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in.

syntax:
- no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> routed-in
- apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in
- no apply policy <POLICY-NAME> in

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command do?

The ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command configures a gateway address for the DHCP relay agent to use for DHCP requests. By default DHCP relay agent picks the lowest-numbered IP address on the interface. The no form of this command removes the gateway address. 364 AOS-CX 10.06 Command-Line Interface Guide. Syntax: no ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR> command is: no ip bootp-gateway <IPv4-ADDR>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command configures one or more domain names that are appended to the DNS request. The DNS client appends each name in succession until the DNS server replies. Domains can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. The no form of this command removes a domain from the list. Syntax: no ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
The syntax of the ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command is: no ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command do?

The ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Syntax: no ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command?
The syntax of the ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command is: no ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command do?

The ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command associates a static IP address with a hostname. The DNS client returns this IP address instead of querying a DNS server for an IP address for the hostname. Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. Chapter 26 DNS client commands 401. Syntax: no ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command?
The syntax of the ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ] command is: no ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> command do?

The erps ring <ringid> command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. This command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. This command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Syntax: no ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command is: no ip igmp apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command do?

The ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command configures an IGMP last member query interval value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the value to a default of 1 second on an interface. Chapter 36 IGMP commands 475. Syntax: no ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE> command is: no ip igmp last-member-query-interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp querier command do?

The ip igmp querier command configures an IGMP querier on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. This functionality will allow an interface to join in the querier-election process. The no form of this command disables IGMP querier on an interface. Configures the interval between IGMP queries on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the IGMP querier interval to the default value of 125 seconds on an interface. Syntax: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier command?
The syntax of the ip igmp querier command is: ip igmp querier interval <INTERVAL-VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command do?

The ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command configures the IGMP querier max response time value in seconds on an interface, depending on the command context you are in. The no form of this command sets the querier max response time value to the default of 10 seconds on an interface. Chapter 36 IGMP commands 477. Syntax: no ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME> command is: no ip igmp querier query-max-response-time <RESPONSE-TIME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command do?

The ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command configures IGMP robustness on an interface, depending on the command context. The robustness parameter allows tuning for the expected packet loss on a subnet. The no form of this command sets the robustness value to the default of 2 on an interface. Syntax: no ip igmp robustness <VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp robustness <VALUE> command is: no ip igmp robustness <VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command do?

The ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command configures the ACL on a particular interface to filter the IGMP join or leave packets based on rules set in the particular ACL name. The no form of this command unconfigures the rules set for the ACL. 498 AOS-CX 10.06 Command-Line Interface Guide NOTE: This configuration will override the ACL associated with IGMP snooping on the corresponding L2 VLAN. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>.

syntax: no ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME> command is: no ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>

syntax: no ip igmp snooping apply access-list <ACL-NAME>

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST> command configures the specified ports in auto mode. In auto mode traffic flow is controlled by the IGMP joins/leaves. Auto mode is the default. The no form of this command removes auto mode ports for the VLAN. Chapter 37 IGMP Snooping commands 499. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST> command is: no ip igmp snooping auto <PORT-LIST>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command enables the port to learn group information when receiving a topology change notification. By default, fast learn is not enabled on ports. The no form of this command disables fast learn on the specified ports. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST> command is: no ip igmp snooping fastlearn <PORT-LIST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST> command enables the switch to immediately remove the IGMP client from its IGMP table and cease transmitting multicast traffic to the client. The no form of this command disables fastleave on the specified ports. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST> command is: no ip igmp snooping fastleave <PORT-LIST>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST> command configures the specified ports in forced fastleave mode. The no form of this command disables forced fastleave on the specified ports. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST> command is: no ip igmp snooping forced-fastleave <PORT-LIST>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST> command do?

The ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST> command configures the specified ports in forward mode. In forward mode, traffic is always forwarded on this port, irrespective of joins. The no form of this command disables forward ports. Syntax: no ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST> command?
The syntax of the ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST> command is: no ip igmp snooping forward <PORT-LIST>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback <ID> command do?

The interface loopback <ID> command creates a loopback interface and changes to the config-loopback-if context. Loopback interfaces are layer 3. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: no interface loopback <ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback <ID> command?
The syntax of the interface loopback <ID> command is: no interface loopback <ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command do?

The interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command creates an interface VLAN also know as an SVI (switched virtual interface) and changes to the config-if- vlan context. The specified VLAN must already be defined on the switch. The no form of this command deletes an interface VLAN. Syntax: no interface vlan <VLAN-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command?
The syntax of the interface vlan <VLAN-ID> command is: no interface vlan <VLAN-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME> command?
The syntax of the ip-sla <IP-SLA-NAME> command is: ip-sla responder <SLA-NAME> {udp-echo | tcp-connect | udp-jitter-voip} <PORT-NUM>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command do?

The interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command creates or updates an IP tunnel. After you enter the command, the firmware switches to the configuration context for the tunnel. If the specified tunnel exists, this command switches to the context for the tunnel. By default, all tunnels are automatically assigned to the default VRF when they are created. The no form of this command deletes an existing IP tunnel. Chapter 44 IP tunnels commands 567. Syntax: no interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command?
The syntax of the interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER> command is: no interface tunnel <EXISTING-TUNNEL-NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command do?

The ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command sets the local IP address of a GRE tunnel. This address identifies the tunnel interface for routing. It must be on the same subnet as the tunnel address assigned on the remote device. The no form of this command deletes the local IP address assigned to a GRE tunnel. Syntax: no ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command?
The syntax of the ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> command is: no ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface lag <ID> command do?

The interface lag <ID> command creates a Link Aggregation Group (LAG) interface represented by an ID. The no form of this command deletes a LAG interface represented by an ID. Syntax: no interface lag <ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface lag <ID> command?
The syntax of the interface lag <ID> command is: no interface lag <ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ssh password-authentication command do?

The ssh password-authentication command enables the password-based authentication method for use with SSH clients. The no form of this command disables the password-based authentication method for use with SSH clients. Syntax: no ssh password-authentication.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ssh password-authentication command?
The syntax of the ssh password-authentication command is: no ssh password-authentication.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback <INSTANCE> command do?

The interface loopback <INSTANCE> command creates a loopback interface and enters loopback configuration mode. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: no interface loopback <INSTANCE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback <INSTANCE> command?
The syntax of the interface loopback <INSTANCE> command is: no interface loopback <INSTANCE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no ntp server <IP-ADDR> command do?

The no ntp server <IP-ADDR> command defines an NTP server to use for time synchronization, or updates the settings of an existing server with new values. Up to eight servers can be defined. The no form of this command removes a configured NTP server. Syntax: no ntp server <IP-ADDR> [burst] [iburst] [prefer] [key-id <KEY-NUM>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no ntp server <IP-ADDR> command?
The syntax of the no ntp server <IP-ADDR> command is: no ntp server <IP-ADDR> [burst] [iburst] [prefer] [key-id <KEY-NUM>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command do?

The ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command runs the OSPF protocol on the interface with the configured IPv4 address for the area specified. The interfaces which have an IP address configured in this network or in a subset of this network, will participate in the OSPF protocol. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Sets the authentication type that will be used for authentication with the neighbor router. Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command?
The syntax of the ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command is: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol Configures null authentication on an interface which disables IPsec authentication. Configures IPSec AH authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. Sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID> command is: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command do?

The crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command creates a leaf certificate and changes to its context config-cert-<CERT-NAME>. If the specified leaf certificate exists, this command changes to its context. The first time the switch starts it creates a self-signed, default leaf certificate called local-cert. This certificate is used by any switch application that does not have an associated leaf certificate. The no form of this command deletes the specified leaf certificate. The default leaf certificate local-cert cannot be deleted. Syntax: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command?
The syntax of the crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME> command is: no crypto pki certificate <CERT-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the qos queue-profile <NAME> command do?

The qos queue-profile <NAME> command creates a new QoS queue profile and switches to the config-queue context for the profile. Or, if the specified QoS queue profile exists, this command switches to the config-queue context for the profile. A queue profile maps queues to local-priority values. Each profile has eight queues, numbered 0 to 7. The larger the queue number, the higher its priority during transmission scheduling. A queue profile named factory-default is defined by default and is automatically applied to all interfaces. It cannot be edited or deleted. Use the command show qos queue-profile factory- default to view this profile. Do not use show running-configuration, as it will only display changes from the initial values. The no form of this command removes the specified QoS queue profile. Syntax: no qos queue-profile <NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the qos queue-profile <NAME> command?
The syntax of the qos queue-profile <NAME> command is: no qos queue-profile <NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip rip all-ip enable command do?

The ip rip all-ip enable command enables RIP process for all RIP enabled IP addresses configured on interface. The no form of this command disables RIP process on the interface. Syntax: no ip rip all-ip enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip rip all-ip enable command?
The syntax of the ip rip all-ip enable command is: no ip rip all-ip enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip rip all-ip disable command do?

The ip rip all-ip disable command disables RIP process for all RIP enabled IP addresses configured on the interface. The no form of this command enables RIP process for all RIP enabled IP addresses configured on the interface. Syntax: no ip rip all-ip disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip rip all-ip disable command?
The syntax of the ip rip all-ip disable command is: no ip rip all-ip disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip rip all-ip send disable command do?

The ip rip all-ip send disable command disables interface from sending RIP packets for all RIP enabled IP addresses. The no form of this command enables interface to send RIP packets for all RIP enabled IP addresses. Syntax: no ip rip all-ip send disable.

syntax: no ip rip all-ip send disable

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip rip all-ip send disable command?
The syntax of the ip rip all-ip send disable command is: no ip rip all-ip send disable.

syntax: no ip rip all-ip send disable

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command do?

The router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command creates RIPng process if not already created and enters the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> context for the VRF mentioned. If no VRF is mentioned, a default is used. Only one RIPng process is allowed per VRF. The no form of this command deletes the RIPng instance for the VRF. If no VRF is mentioned the default is deleted. Syntax: no router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command?
The syntax of the router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>] command is: no router ripng <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vrf command do?
The vrf command is documented in this guide. Syntax: Sets VRF name for RIPng process.; <VRF-NAME>; VRF name for VRF.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vrf command?
The syntax of the vrf command is: Sets VRF name for RIPng process.; <VRF-NAME>; VRF name for VRF.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command do?

The vlan <VLAN-LIST> command creates a VLAN and changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. By default, the VLAN is enabled. To disable a VLAN, use the no shutdown command. If the specified VLAN exists, this command changes to the config-vlan-id context for the VLAN. If a range of VLANs is specified, the context does not change. The no form of this command removes a VLAN. VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and cannot be deleted. Creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface and change the interface to a layer 2 interface. Syntax: vlan access <VLAN-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command?
The syntax of the vlan <VLAN-LIST> command is: vlan access <VLAN-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no routing command do?
The no routing command is documented in this guide. Syntax: no routing.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no routing command?
The syntax of the no routing command is: no routing.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync command do?
The vsx-sync command is documented in this guide. Syntax: access-list ip test1; vsx-sync; ! ; policy test2.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync command?
The syntax of the vsx-sync command is: access-list ip test1; vsx-sync; ! ; policy test2.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx command do?
The vsx command creates the VSX context on the switch. The no form of this command disables the VSX context on the switch and removes all related configuration settings. Syntax: no vsx.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx command?
The syntax of the vsx command is: no vsx.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx active-forwarding command do?

The vsx active-forwarding command configures VSX active-forwarding on an interface VLAN. The no form of this command unconfigures VSX active-forwarding on a VLAN interface. Syntax: no vsx active-forwarding.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx active-forwarding command?
The syntax of the vsx active-forwarding command is: no vsx active-forwarding.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]} command do?

The vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]} command enables VSX synchronization for the following for a logical interface or a LAG instance: • Access lists • IRDP configurations • QoS • Rate limits • Port filter configurations • VLAN associations This command enables VSX synchronization for individual associations and to the combination of associations to the interface context. To synchronize the associations, you must configure the same interface on the peer switch. Chapter 101 VSX commands 1495 IMPORTANT: When enabling VSX synchronization under a physical interface, under a VLAN interface, or a VSX LAG, create on the secondary switch the physical interface, VLAN interface, or VSX LAG with the same name and routing setting as on the primary switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]} command?

The syntax of the vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]} command is: no vsx-sync {[access-lists] [qos] [rate-limits] [vlans] [policies] [irdp] [portfilter]}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command do?

The vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command enables VSX sync of active gateways or policies associated under an interface. To synchronize the associations, you must configure the same interface vlan on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization for active gateways or policies associated under an interface, but it does not remove the feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command?
The syntax of the vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]} command is: no vsx-sync {[active-gateways] [policies]}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync aaa command do?

The vsx-sync aaa command enables VSX synchronization of all AAA configurations, including user, RADIUS server, and TACACS+ server, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. To synchronize AAA configurations associated with a particular VRF, you must configure the same VRF on the peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global AAA configurations, but it does not remove the existing global AAA feature configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync aaa.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync aaa command?
The syntax of the vsx-sync aaa command is: no vsx-sync aaa.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync acl-log-timer command do?

The vsx-sync acl-log-timer command enables VSX synchronization of access list log timer configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of access list log timer configurations to the secondary peer. However, it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync acl-log-timer.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync acl-log-timer command?
The syntax of the vsx-sync acl-log-timer command is: no vsx-sync acl-log-timer.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync arp-security command do?

The vsx-sync arp-security command enables VSX synchronization of the ARP security configurations on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. After you enter vsx-sync arp-security, you must enter vsx-sync mclag-interfaces for enabling VSX synchronization for the ARP security feature. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of ARP security configurations on VLAN mode and LAG interface mode to the secondary peer switch. However, it does not remove the existing ARP security configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync arp-security.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync arp-security command?
The syntax of the vsx-sync arp-security command is: no vsx-sync arp-security.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync bfd-global command do?

The vsx-sync bfd-global command enables syncing of global BFD configurations, such as echo-src-ip-address, detect-multiplier, min- transmit-interval, and min-receive-interval, on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. IMPORTANT: This command enables VSX synchronization only at the top level and not at the context level. The no form of this command disables the syncing of global BFD configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the existing global BFD feature configurations from it. Syntax: no vsx-sync bfd-global.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync bfd-global command?
The syntax of the vsx-sync bfd-global command is: no vsx-sync bfd-global.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync bgp command do?

The vsx-sync bgp command enables syncing of BGP configurations on the primary VSX switch to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command disables syncing BGP, as path lists, community lists, prefix lists, and route map configurations to the secondary peer, but it does not remove the previously synced configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync bgp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync bgp command?
The syntax of the vsx-sync bgp command is: no vsx-sync bgp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync copp-policy command do?

The vsx-sync copp-policy command enables VSX synchronization of CoPP policy configurations on the primary VSX node to the secondary peer switch. The no form of this command removes VSX synchronization of global CoPP configurations, but it does not remove the existing global CoPP configurations from the secondary peer switch. Syntax: no vsx-sync copp-policy.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync copp-policy command?
The syntax of the vsx-sync copp-policy command is: no vsx-sync copp-policy.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vsx-sync dcb-global command do?

The vsx-sync dcb-global command enables VSX synchronization of global DCBx configurations from the primary VSX node to the secondary peer. The no form of the command disables VSX synchronization of global DCBx configurations to the secondary peer; however, it does not remove the existing DCBx feature configurations from the secondary peer. Syntax: no vsx-sync dcb-global.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vsx-sync dcb-global command?

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure CoPP?

To configure CoPP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure the default CoPP policy, edit an existing policy, or create a policy with the command copp- 2. Add, edit, or remove classes in the policy with the command class 3. If the policy is not the active policy on the switch, apply it with the command apply copp-policy 4. Review the CoPP policy configuration settings with the command show copp-policy. Commands shown in the procedure include: policy..
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the apply copp-policy command do?

The apply copp-policy command applies a CoPP policy to the switch, replacing the policy that is in effect. There may be a brief interruption in traffic flow to the management processor while the switch implements the change. Enter the no apply copp-policy <NAME> command with the name of a CoPP policy to unapply a CoPP policy and apply the default CoPP policy. This will only take effect if the specified policy is actively applied. Since there must always be a CoPP policy applied, this command effectively attempts to replace the applied CoPP policy with the default CoPP policy. The default CoPP policy cannot be unapplied using this command. Syntax: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the apply copp-policy command?
The syntax of the apply copp-policy command is: apply copp-policy { <NAME> | default }.

The syntax of the class command is: class <CLASS> {drop | priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>]}.

The clear copp-policy statistics command resets statistics for all CoPP classes to zero. Syntax: clear copp-policy statistics.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command do?

The default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>] command configures the default class that is automatically defined for all CoPP policies. The default class cannot be removed, but its configuration can be changed. The default class is applied to traffic that does not match any other class defined for a policy. Resets an active CoPP policy to match the settings that are currently in effect for the active policy on the switch. Changes made to the active policy that could not be activated are removed from the active policy. 14 AOS-CX 10.06 CoPP Guide When the switch fails to add or modify a class in an active CoPP policy, it is possible the active policy settings on the switch may be out of sync with those defined in the policy. Syntax: default-class priority <PRIORITY> rate <RATE> [burst <BURST>].

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Management of rotated log files?

Management of rotated log files: Rotated log files are compressed and stored locally in /var/log/, regardless of the remote host configuration. Rotated log files are stored with respective time extension to the granularity of hour in the format file1-YYYYMMDDHH.gz (for example, messages-2015080715.gz). Rotated log files are replaced when the number of old rotated log files exceeds three. The newly rotated log file replaces the oldest rotated log file. Remote transfer of rotated log files Only the TFTP protocol is supported for remote transfer, and both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses are supported. Only newly rotated log files are transferred to the remote host during the log rotation. Previously rotated log files are not transferred. After a file is successfully transferred, it is removed from the switch local path.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command do?

The logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command specifies the maximum allowed log file size. The no form of this command resets the size of the log file to the default (100 MB). Sets the rotate period for the event logs, stored in the /var/log/event.log file, and authentication logs, stored in the /var/log/auth.log file. Defaults to daily. A log file that exceeds either the logrotate <MAX-SIZE> value or the logrotate period (whichever happens first), triggers rotation for that log file. The no form of this command resets the log rotation period to the default. Syntax: logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE>.

The syntax of the logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE> command is: logrotate maxsize <MAX-SIZE>

Initializing The module is being initialized. Present The module hardware is installed in the chassis. Ready The module is available for use. Updating A firmware update is being applied to the module. Examples Showing all installed modules: switch(config)# show module.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot?

To troubleshoot, follow the documented procedure: 1. Modify the ACL to allow the ping traffic 2. Unapply the ACL from egress (8400/8320/8325 switches) or Control Plane 3. Ping a destination which is not matched by the ACL. For example, if the ACL is blocking traffic based on. Commands shown in the procedure include: PING 100.1.2.10 (100.1.2.10) 100(128) bytes of data; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted; ping: sendmsg: Operation not permitted.

ISP is intended to run automatically either at boot time or as new modules are inserted into the chassis at runtime. section: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues?

To troubleshoot Web UI and REST API access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.

task: Troubleshooting Web UI and REST API access issues

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about About AOS-CX?

About AOS-CX: AOS-CX introduction AOS-CX is a new, modern, fully programmable operating system built using a database-centric design that ensures higher availability and dynamic software process changes for reduced downtime. In addition to robust hardware reliability, the AOS-CX operating system includes additional software elements not available with traditional systems, including: • Automated visibility to help IT organizations scale: The Aruba Network Analytics Engine allows IT to monitor and troubleshoot network, system, application, and security-related issues easily through simple scripts. This engine comes with a built-in time series database that enables customers and developers to create software modules that allow historical troubleshooting, as well as analysis of historical trends to predict and avoid future problems due to scale, security, and performance bottlenecks.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Initial configuration?

Initial configuration: Perform the initial configuration of a factory default switch using one of the following methods: • Load a switch configuration using zero-touch provisioning (ZTP). When ZTP is used, the configuration is loaded from a server automatically when the switch booted from the factory default configuration. • Connect to the switch wirelessly with a mobile device through Bluetooth, and use the Aruba CX Mobile App to deploy an initial configuration from a provided template. The template you choose during the deployment process determines how the management interface is configured. Optionally, as the final deployment step, you can select to import the switch into Net Edit through a WiFI connection to the Net Edit server. Alternatively, you can use the Aruba CX Mobile App to manually configure switch settings and features for a subset of the features you can configure using the CLI. You can also access the CLI through the mobile application.

section: Configuring in-band management on a data port

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure in-band management on a data port?

To configure in-band management on a data port, follow the documented procedure: 1. Use an Ethernet cable to connect a data port to your network 2. Configure a layer 3 interface on the data port 3. Enable SSH support on the interface (on the default VRF) with the command ssh server vrf default 4. Enable the Web UI on the interface (on the default VRF) with the command https-server vrf 5. Log in to the CLI 6. Switch to config context and enable the Web UI on the management port VRF with the command 7. Start your web browser and enter the IP address of the management port in the address bar, 8. The Web UI starts and you are prompted to log in. Commands shown in the procedure include: https-server vrf mgmt..

task: Configuring in-band management on a data port

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the management interface?

To configure the management interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the management interface context with the command interface mgmt 2. By default, the management interface on the management port is enabled. If it was disabled, reenable it 3. Use the command ip dhcp to configure the management interface to automatically obtain an address 4. SSH is enabled by default on the management VRF. If disabled, enable SSH with the command ssh.

The ip static command is documented in this guide. Syntax: ip static <IP-ADDR>/<MASK>.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication command do?
The ntp authentication command enables support for authentication when communicating with an NTP server. The no form of this command disables authentication support. Syntax: ntp authentication.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication command?
The syntax of the ntp authentication command is: ntp authentication.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp authentication-key command do?

The ntp authentication-key command defines an authentication key that is used to secure the exchange with an NTP time server. This command provides protection against accidentally synchronizing to a time source that is not trusted. The no form of this command removes an authentication key. Syntax: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp authentication-key command?
The syntax of the ntp authentication-key command is: ntp authentication-key <KEY-ID> {md5 | sha1} <PASSWORD> [trusted]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp disable command do?
The ntp disable command disables the NTP client on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. Syntax: ntp disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp disable command?
The syntax of the ntp disable command is: ntp disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp enable command do?

The ntp enable command enables the NTP client on the switch to automatically adjust the local time and date on the switch. The NTP client is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables the NTP client. Syntax: ntp enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp enable command?

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp server command do?

The ntp server command defines an NTP server to use for time synchronization, or updates the settings of an existing server with new values. Up to eight servers can be defined. The no form of this command removes a configured NTP server. Syntax: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp server command?
The syntax of the ntp server command is: ntp server <IP-ADDR> [key <KEY-NUM>] [minpoll <MIN-NUM>] [maxpoll <MAX-NUM>][burst | iburst] [prefer] [version <VER-NUM>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp trusted-key command do?

The ntp trusted-key command sets a key as trusted. When NTP authentication is enabled, the switch only synchronizes with time servers that transmit packets containing a trusted key. The no form of this command removes the trusted designation from a key. Syntax: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp trusted-key command?
The syntax of the ntp trusted-key command is: ntp trusted-key <KEY-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ntp vrf command do?
The ntp vrf command specifies the VRF on which the NTP client communicates with an NTP server. The switch cannot function as both NTP master and client on the same VRF. Syntax: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ntp vrf command?
The syntax of the ntp vrf command is: ntp vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp associations command do?

The show ntp associations command shows the status of the connection to each NTP server. The following information is displayed for each server: • Tally code: The first character is the Tally code: ◦ ◦ ◦ (blank): No state information available (e.g. non-responding server) x: Out of tolerance (discarded by intersection algorithm).: Discarded by table overflow (not used). Syntax: show ntp associations [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp associations command?
The syntax of the show ntp associations command is: show ntp associations [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp authentication-keys command?
The syntax of the show ntp authentication-keys command is: show ntp authentication-keys [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp servers command do?
The show ntp servers command shows all configured NTP servers. Syntax: show ntp servers [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp servers command?
The syntax of the show ntp servers command is: show ntp servers [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp statistics command do?

The show ntp statistics command shows global NTP statistics. The following information is displayed: • Rx-pkts: Total NTP packets received. • Current Version Rx-pkts: Number of NTP packets that match the current NTP version. • Old Version Rx-pkts: Number of NTP packets that match the previous NTP version. • Error pkts: Packets dropped due to all other error reasons. • Auth-failed pkts: Packets dropped due to authentication failure. • Declined pkts: Packets denied access for any reason. • Restricted pkts: Packets dropped due to NTP access control. • Rate-limited pkts: Number of packets discarded due to rate limitation. • KOD pkts: Number of Kiss of Death packets sent. Syntax: show ntp statistics [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp statistics command?
The syntax of the show ntp statistics command is: show ntp statistics [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ntp status command do?
The show ntp status command shows the status of NTP on the switch. Syntax: show ntp status [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ntp status command?
The syntax of the show ntp status command is: show ntp status [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show profiles available command do?
The show profiles available command shows all available profiles for the 8320, 8325, and 8360. Syntax: show profiles available.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show profiles available command?
The syntax of the show profiles available command is: show profiles available.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show profile current command do?
The show profile current command shows current profile for 8320 and 8325 switch series. Syntax: show profile current.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show profile current command?
The syntax of the show profile current command is: show profile current.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a layer 2 interface?

To configure a layer 2 interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Change to the interface configuration context for the interface with the command interface 2. By default, interfaces are layer 3. To create a layer 2 interface, disable routing with the command no 3. Set the interface MTU (maximum transmission unit) with the command mtu 4. Review interface configuration settings with the command show interface.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure a layer 3 interface?

To configure a layer 3 interface, follow the documented procedure: 1. Change to the interface configuration context for the interface with the command interface 2. Interfaces are layer 3 by default. If you previously set the interface to layer 2, then enable routing support 3. Assign an IPv4 address with the command ip address, or an IPv6 address with the command IPv6 4. If required, enable support for layer 3 counters with the command l3-counters 5. If required, set the IP MTU with the command ip mtu 6. Review interface configuration settings with the command show interface.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command do?

The default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command sets an interface (or a range of interfaces) to factory default values. Associates descriptive information with an interface to help administrators and operators identify the purpose or role of an interface. The no form of this command removes a description from an interface. Enables negotiation of receive flow control on the current interface. The switch advertises rx support to the link partner. The final configuration is determined based on the capabilities of both partners. Priority-based flow control (PFC), on the 8325, takes effect after the configuration is saved to startup-config and the switch is restarted. Syntax: default interface <INTERFACE-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command?
The syntax of the default interface <INTERFACE-ID> command is: default interface <INTERFACE-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface command do?

The interface command switches to the config-if context for a physical port. This is where you define the configuration settings for the logical interface associated with the physical port. 48 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide. Syntax: interface <PORT-NUM>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface command?
The syntax of the interface command is: interface <PORT-NUM>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the interface loopback command do?

The interface loopback command creates a loopback interface and changes to the config-loopback-if context. Loopback interfaces are layer 3. The no form of this command deletes a loopback interface. Syntax: interface loopback <ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the interface loopback command?
The syntax of the interface loopback command is: interface loopback <ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip address command do?

The ip address command sets an IPv4 address for the current layer 3 interface. The no form of this command removes the IPv4 address from the interface. Syntax: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip address command?
The syntax of the ip address command is: ip address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [secondary]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip mtu command do?

The ip mtu command sets the IP MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the largest IP packet that can be sent or received by the interface. The no form of this command sets the IP MTU to the default value 1500. Syntax: ip mtu <VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip mtu command?
The syntax of the ip mtu command is: ip mtu <VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 address command?
The syntax of the IPv6 address command is: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK>{eui64 | [tag <ID>]}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the mtu <VALUE> command do?

The mtu <VALUE> command enables counters on a layer 3 interface. By default, all interfaces are layer 3. To change a layer 2 interface to layer 3, use the routing command. The no form of this command, with no specification, disables both transmit and receive counters on a layer 3 interface. To disable transmit (tx) or receive (rx) counters only, specify the counter type you want to disable. Sets the MTU (maximum transmission unit) for an interface. This defines the maximum size of a layer 2 (Ethernet) frame. Frames larger than the MTU (1500 bytes by default) are dropped and cause an ICMP fragmentation-needed message to be sent back to the originator. To support jumbo frames (frames larger than 1522 bytes), increase the MTU as required by your network. A frame size of up to 9198 bytes is supported. Syntax: mtu <VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the mtu <VALUE> command?
The syntax of the mtu <VALUE> command is: mtu <VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command do?
The show allow-unsupported-transceiver command displays configuration and status of unsupported transceivers. Syntax: show allow-unsupported-transceiver.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command?
The syntax of the show allow-unsupported-transceiver command is: show allow-unsupported-transceiver.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface command do?
The show interface command displays active configurations and operational status information for interfaces. Syntax: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical | extended [non-zero]].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface command?
The syntax of the show interface command is: show interface [<IFNNAME>|<IFRANGE>] [brief | physical | extended [non-zero]]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface dom command do?

The show interface dom command shows diagnostics information and alarm/warning flags for the optical transceivers (SFP, SFP+, QSFP+). This information is known as DOM (Digital Optical Monitoring). DOM information also consists of vendor determined thresholds which trigger high/low alarms and warning flags. Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail] [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface dom command?
The syntax of the show interface dom command is: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] dom [detail] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface transceiver command do?
The show interface transceiver command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] transceiver [detail | threshold-violations] [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface transceiver command?
The syntax of the show interface transceiver command is: show interface [<INTERFACE-ID>] transceiver [detail | threshold-violations] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip interface command do?
The show ip interface command shows status and configuration information for an IPv4 interface. Syntax: show ip interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip interface command?
The syntax of the show ip interface command is: show ip interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip source-interface command?
The syntax of the show ip source-interface command is: show ip source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}.

syntax: show ip source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 interface command do?
The show IPv6 interface command shows status and configuration information for an IPv6 interface. Syntax: show IPv6 interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer].
