The syntax of the show IPv6 interface command is: show IPv6 interface <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 source-interface command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 source-interface command is: show IPv6 source-interface {sflow | tftp | radius | tacacs | all}.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the shutdown command do?
The shutdown command disables an interface. Interfaces are disabled by default when created. The no form of this command enables an interface. Syntax: shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the shutdown command?
The syntax of the shutdown command is: shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about VLANs?

VLANs: are primarily used to provide network segmentation at layer 2. VLANs enable the grouping of users by logical function instead of physical location. They make managing bandwidth usage within networks possible by: • Allowing grouping of high-bandwidth users on low-traffic segments • Organizing users from different LAN segments according to their need for common resources and individual protocols • Improving traffic control at the edge of networks by separating traffic of different protocol types. • Enhancing network security by creating subnets to control in-band access to specific network resources VLANs are generally assigned on an organizational basis rather than on a physical basis. For example, a network administrator could assign all workstations and servers used by a particular workgroup to the same VLAN, regardless of their physical locations. Hosts in the same VLAN can directly communicate with one another. A router or a Layer 3 switch is required for hosts in different VLANs to communicate with one another.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you create and enabling a VLAN?
To create and enabling a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to the configuration context with the command config 2. Create a new VLAN with the command vlan.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you disable a VLAN?

To disable a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. Switch to configuration context with the command config 2. Switch to configuration context for the VLAN you want to disable with the command vlan 3. Disable the VLAN with the command shutdown.

section: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Assigning a VLAN to an interface?

Assigning a VLAN to an interface: To use a VLAN, it must be assigned to an interface on the switch. VLANs can only be assigned to non-routed (layer 2) interfaces. All interfaces are routed (layer 3) by default when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface.

topic: Assigning a VLAN to an interface

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show capacities svi-count command do?
The show capacities svi-count command shows the maximum number of SVIs supported by the switch. Syntax: show capacities svi-count.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show capacities svi-count command?
The syntax of the show capacities svi-count command is: show capacities svi-count.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show system internal-vlan-range command do?
The show system internal-vlan-range command shows the VLAN range reserved for internal use. Syntax: show system internal-vlan-range.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show system internal-vlan-range command?
The syntax of the show system internal-vlan-range command is: show system internal-vlan-range.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command do?
The show vlan [<VLAN-ID>] [vsx-peer] command displays configuration information for all VLANs or a specific VLAN.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan port command do?
The show vlan port command displays the VLANs configured for a specific layer 2 interface. Syntax: show vlan port <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vlan port command?
The syntax of the show vlan port command is: show vlan port <INTERFACE-ID> [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show VLANsummary command do?
The show VLANsummary command displays a summary of the VLAN configuration on the switch. Syntax: show VLANsummary [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show VLANsummary command?
The syntax of the show VLANsummary command is: show VLANsummary [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show vlan translation command do?

The show vlan translation command shows a summary of all VLAN translations rules defined on the switch, or the rules defined for a specific interface. Syntax: show vlan translation [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show vlan translation command?
The syntax of the show vlan translation command is: show vlan translation [interface <INTERFACE-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan access [<VLAN-ID>] command creates an access interface and assigns an VLAN ID to it. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned to each access interface. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface and change the interface to a layer 2 interface. The no form of this command removes an access VLAN from the interface in the current context and sets it to the default VLAN ID of 1.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan translate <VLAN-1> <VLAN-2> command do?

The no vlan translate <VLAN-1> <VLAN-2> command defines a bidirectional VLAN translation rule that maps an external VLAN ID to an internal VLAN ID on a LAG or layer 2 interface. Applies to both incoming and outgoing traffic. The no form of this command removes an existing VLAN translation rule on the current interface. NOTE: VLAN translation and MVRP cannot be enabled on the same interface.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command do?

The no vlan trunk allowed [<VLAN-LIST>] command assigns a VLAN ID to an trunk interface. Multiple VLAN IDs can be assigned to a trunk interface. These VLAN IDs define which VLAN traffic is allowed across the trunk interface. VLANs can be assigned only to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. By default, all interfaces are routed (layer 3) when created. Use the no routing command to disable routing on an interface. The no form of this command removes one or more VLAN IDs from a trunk interface. When the last VLAN is removed from a trunk interface, the interface continues to operate in trunk mode, and will trunk all the VLANs currently defined on the switch, and any new VLANs defined in the future. To disable the trunk interface, use the command shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command do?

The no vlan trunk native [<VLAN-ID>] command assigns a native VLAN ID to a trunk interface. By default, VLAN ID 1 is assigned as the native VLAN ID for all trunk interfaces. VLANs can only be assigned to a non-routed (layer 2) interface or LAG interface. Only one VLAN ID can be assigned as the native VLAN. NOTE: When a native VLAN is defined, the switch automatically executes the vlan trunk allowed all command to ensure that the default VLAN is allowed on the trunk. To only allow specific VLANs on the trunk, issue the vlan trunk allowed command specifying only specific VLANs. The no form of this command removes a native VLAN from a trunk interface and assigns VLAN ID 1 as its native VLAN.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command do?

The no vlan trunk native <VLAN-ID> tag command enables tagging on a native VLAN. Only incoming packets that are tagged with the matching VLAN ID are accepted. Incoming packets that are untagged are dropped except for BPDUs. Egress packets are tagged. The no form of this command removes tagging on a native VLAN.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the voice command do?
The voice command configures a VLAN as a voice VLAN. The no form of this command removes voice configuration from a VLAN. Syntax: voice.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the voice command?
The syntax of the voice command is: voice.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Configuration and firmware management?

Configuration and firmware management: Checkpoints A checkpoint is a snapshot of the running configuration of a switch and its relevant metadata during the time of creation. Checkpoints can be used to apply the switch configuration stored within a checkpoint whenever needed, such as to revert to a previous, clean configuration. Checkpoints can be applied to other switches of the same platform. A switch is able to store multiple checkpoints. Checkpoint types The switch supports two types of checkpoints: • System generated checkpoints: The switch automatically generates a system checkpoint whenever a configuration change occurs. • User generated checkpoints: The administrator can manually generate a checkpoint whenever required. Maximum number of checkpoints • Maximum checkpoints: 64 (including the startup configuration) • Maximum user checkpoints: 32 • Maximum system checkpoints: 32 User generated checkpoints User checkpoints can be created at any time, as long as one configuration difference exists since the last checkpoint was created.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto command do?

The checkpoint auto command starts auto checkpoint mode. In auto checkpoint mode, the switch temporarily saves the runtime configuration as a checkpoint only for the specified time lapse interval. Configuration changes must be saved before the interval expires, otherwise the runtime configuration is restored from the temporary checkpoint. Syntax: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto command?
The syntax of the checkpoint auto command is: checkpoint auto <TIME-LAPSE-INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint auto confirm command do?

The checkpoint auto confirm command signals to the switch to save the running configuration used during the auto checkpoint mode. This command also ends the auto checkpoint mode. Syntax: checkpoint auto confirm.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint auto confirm command?
The syntax of the checkpoint auto confirm command is: checkpoint auto confirm.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint diff command do?

The checkpoint diff command shows the difference in configuration between two configurations. Compare checkpoints, the running configuration, or the startup configuration. Syntax: checkpoint diff {<CHECKPOINT-NAME1> | running-config | startup-config}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint diff command?
The syntax of the checkpoint diff command is: checkpoint diff {<CHECKPOINT-NAME1> | running-config | startup-config}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command?
The syntax of the checkpoint post-configuration timeout command is: checkpoint post-configuration timeout <TIMEOUT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint rename command do?
The checkpoint rename command renames an existing checkpoint. Syntax: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME> <NEW-CHECKPOINT-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rename command?
The syntax of the checkpoint rename command is: checkpoint rename <OLD-CHECKPOINT-NAME> <NEW-CHECKPOINT-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the checkpoint rollback command do?

The checkpoint rollback command applies the configuration from a pre-existing checkpoint or the startup configuration to the running configuration. Syntax: checkpoint rollback {<CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the checkpoint rollback command?
The syntax of the checkpoint rollback command is: checkpoint rollback {<CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command do?

The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command copies a checkpoint configuration to a remote location as a file. The configuration is exported in checkpoint format, which includes switch configuration and relevant metadata. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <REMOTE-URL> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {running-config startup-config} command?
The syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {running-config startup-config} command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> {running-config | startup-config}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command do?

The copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command copies an existing checkpoint configuration to a USB drive. The file format is defined when the checkpoint was created. Syntax: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL> command is: copy checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> <STORAGE-URL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command do?

The copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command copies a remote configuration file to a checkpoint. The remote configuration file must be in checkpoint format. Syntax: copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command?
The syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command is: copy <REMOTE-URL> checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> {running-config startup-config} command?
The syntax of the copy <REMOTE-URL> {running-config startup-config} command is: copy <REMOTE-URL> {running-config | startup-config } [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy running-config {startup-config checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>} command?
The syntax of the copy running-config {startup-config checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>} command is: copy running-config {startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <REMOTE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <REMOTE-URL> command is: copy {running-config | startup-config} <REMOTE-URL> {cli | json} [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <STORAGE-URL> command?
The syntax of the copy {running-config startup-config} <STORAGE-URL> command is: copy {running-config | startup-config} <STORAGE-URL> {cli | json}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy startup-config running-config command do?
The copy startup-config running-config command copies the startup configuration to the running configuration. Syntax: copy startup-config running-config.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy startup-config running-config command?
The syntax of the copy startup-config running-config command is: copy startup-config running-config.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command do?
The copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command is documented in this guide. Syntax: copy <STORAGE-URL> {running-config | startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command?
The syntax of the copy <STORAGE-URL> running-config command is: copy <STORAGE-URL> {running-config | startup-config | checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erase {checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> startup-config all} command?
The syntax of the erase {checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> startup-config all} command is: erase {checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> | startup-config | all}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command do?
The show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command shows the configuration of a checkpoint. Syntax: show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [json].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command?
The syntax of the show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> command is: show checkpoint <CHECKPOINT-NAME> [json]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show checkpoint list command do?
The show checkpoint list command shows a list of saved checkpoints. Syntax: show checkpoint list {all | <START-DATE> <END-DATE>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show checkpoint list command?
The syntax of the show checkpoint list command is: show checkpoint list {all | <START-DATE> <END-DATE>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot set-default command do?
The boot set-default command sets the default operating system image to use when the system is booted. Syntax: boot set-default {primary | secondary}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the boot set-default command?
The syntax of the boot set-default command is: boot set-default {primary | secondary}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the boot system command do?

The boot system command reboots all modules on the switch. By default, the configured default operating system image is used. Optional parameters enable you to specify which system image to use for the reboot operation and for future reboot operations. Syntax: boot system [primary | secondary | serviceos].

The snmp-server community command adds an SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. A community string is a password that controls read access to the SNMP agent. A network management program must supply this name when attempting to get SNMP information from the switch. A maximum of 10 community strings are supported. Once you create your own community string, the default community string (public) is deleted. The no form of this command removes the specified SNMP v1/SNMP v2c community string. When no community string exists, a default community string with the value public is automatically defined. Syntax: snmp-server community <STRING>.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Aruba Central Integration?

Aruba Central Integration: The Aruba Central network management solution, a software-as-a-service subscription in the cloud, provides streamlined management of multiple network devices. Arub a OS-CX switches are able to talk to Aruba Central and utilize cloud-based management functionality. Cloud-based management functionality allows for the deployment of network devices at sites with no or few dedicated IT personnel (branch offices, retail stores, and so forth). Arub a OS-CX switches utilize secure communication protocols to connect to the Aruba Central cloud portal, and can coexist with corporate security standards, such as those mandating the use of firewalls. This feature provides: • Zero-touch provisioning.

The aruba-central command creates or enters the Aruba Central configuration context (config-aruba-central). Disables connection to Aruba Central server. It also disconnects any active connection to the Aruba Central server. Syntax: aruba-central.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is DNS?

DNS: The Domain Name System (DNS) is the Internet protocol for mapping a hostname to its IP address. DNS allows users to enter more readily memorable and intuitive hostnames, rather than IP addresses, to identify devices connected to a network. It also allows a host to keep the same hostname even if it changes its IP address. Hostname resolution can be either static or dynamic. • In static resolution, a local table is defined on the switch that associates hostnames with their IP addresses. Static tables can be used to speed up the resolution of frequently queried hosts. • Dynamic resolution requires that the switch query a DNS server located elsewhere on the network. Dynamic name resolution takes more time than static name resolution, but requires far less configuration and management. DNS client The DNS client resolves hostnames to IP addresses for protocols that are running on the switch. When the DNS client receives a request to resolve a hostname, it can do so in one of two ways: • Forward the request to a DNS name server for resolution.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure the DNS client?

To configure the DNS client, follow the documented procedure: 1. Configure one or more DNS name servers with the command ip dns server 2. To resolve DNS requests by appending a domain name to the requests, either configure a single domain 3. To use static name resolution for certain hosts, associate an IP address to a host with the command ip 4. Review your DNS configuration settings with the command show ip dns. Commands shown in the procedure include: VRF Name: vrf_mgmt; VRF Name: vrf_default; VRF Name: mainvrf.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip dns domain-list command do?

The ip dns domain-list command configures one or more domain names that are appended to the DNS request. The DNS client appends each name in succession until the DNS server replies. Domains can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. The no form of this command removes a domain from the list. Syntax: ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].

The syntax of the ip dns domain-list command is: ip dns domain-list <DOMAIN-NAME> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

The ip dns domain-name command configures a domain name that is appended to the DNS request. The domain can be either IPv4 or IPv6. By default, requests are forwarded on the default VRF. If a domain list is defined with the command ip dns domain-list, the domain name defined with this command is ignored. The no form of this command removes the domain name. Syntax: ip dns domain-name <DOMAIN-NAME> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].

Up to six hosts can be defined. If no VRF is defined, the default VRF is used. The no form of this command removes a static IP address associated with a hostname. Syntax: ip dns host <HOST-NAME> <IP-ADDR> [ vrf <VRF-NAME> ].

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp command do?

The lldp command enables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. By default, LLDP is enabled. The no form of this command disables LLDP support globally on all active interfaces. It does not remove any LLDP configuration settings. Sets the 802.3 TLVs to be advertised. By default, advertisement of both POE and MAC/PHY TLVs is enabled. Not supported on the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables advertisement of 802.3 TLVs. Sets the holdtime that is used to calculate the LLDP Time-to-Live value. Time-to-Live defines the length of time that neighbors consider LLDP information sent by this agent as valid. When Time-to-Live expires, the information is deleted by the neighbor. Time-to-live is calculated by multiplying holdtime by the value of lldp timer. Syntax: lldp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp command?
The syntax of the lldp command is: lldp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp management-IPv6-address <IPv6-ADDR> command do?

The lldp management-IPv6-address <IPv6-ADDR> command defines the IPv4 management address of the switch which is sent in the management address TLV. One IPv4 and one IPv6 management address can be configured. If you do not define an LLDP management address, then LLDP uses one of the following (in order): • • IP address of the port IP address of the management interface • Base MAC address of the switch The no form of this command removes the IPv4 management address of the switch. Defines the IPv6 management address of the switch. The management address is encapsulated in the management address TLV. If no management address is specified, LLDP uses the IP address of the management interface, and if this the management interface is not defined, the chassis ID. The no form of this command removes the IPv6 management address of the switch. Syntax: lldp management-IPv6-address <IPv6-ADDR>.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command do?

The lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr } command configures support for the LLDP-MED TLV. Supports only civic address and emergency location information number (ELIN). Coordinate-based location is not supported. The no form of this command disables support for the LLDP MED TLV. Enables reception of LLDP information on an interface. By default, LLDP reception is enabled on all active interfaces, including the OOBM interface. The no form of this command disables reception of LLDP information on an interface. Sets the amount of time (in seconds) to wait before performing LLDP initialization on an interface. The no form of this command sets the reinitialization time to its default value of 2 seconds. Syntax: lldp med-location {civic-addr | elin-addr }.

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command do?

The lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME> command selects a TLV that the LLDP agent will send and receive. By default, all supported TLVs are sent and received. The no form of this command stops the LLDP agent from sending and receiving a specific TLV. Sets the interval (in seconds) at which local LLDP information is updated and TLVs are sent to neighboring network devices by the LLDP agent. The minimum setting for this timer must be four times the value of lldp txdelay. For example, this is a valid configuration: • lldp timer = 16 • lldp txdelay = 4 And, this is an invalid configuration: • lldp timer = 5 • lldp txdelay = 2. Syntax: lldp select-tlv <TLV-NAME>.

The no form of this command disables transmission of LLDP information on an interface. 188 AOS-CX 10.06 Fundamentals Guide Sets the amount of time (in seconds) to wait before sending LLDP information from any interface. For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Parent Interface: interface 1/1/11?

Parent Interface: interface 1/1/11: Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP) Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP) is a proprietary layer 2 protocol supported by most Cisco devices. It is used to exchange information, such as software version, device capabilities, and voice VLAN information, between directly connected devices, such as a VoIP phone and a switch. CDP support By default, CDP is enabled on each active switch port. This is a read-only capability, which means the switch can receive and store information about adjacent CDP devices, but does not generate CDP packets (except when communicating with Cisco IP phones.) The switch supports CDPv2 only and does not support SNMP MIB traps. When a CDP-enabled port receives a CDP packet from another CDP device, it enters data for that device into the CDP Neighbors table, along with the port number on which the data was received. It does not forward the packet. The switch also periodically purges the table of any entries that have expired.
