For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp command do?
The show cdp command shows CDP information for all interfaces. Syntax: show cdp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp command?
The syntax of the show cdp command is: show cdp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show cdp neighbor-info command do?
The show cdp neighbor-info command shows CDP information for all neighbors or for CDP information on a specific interface. Syntax: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show cdp neighbor-info command?
The syntax of the show cdp neighbor-info command is: show cdp neighbor-info <INTERFACE-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about DCBx?

DCBx: is a discovery and capability exchange protocol to discover peers and negotiate Data Center Bridging configuration. DCBx is specified as part of IEEE 802.1Qaz-2011. DCBx uses LLDP as the underlying protocol for exchange of parameters with the peer. The DCBx parameters are exchanged as LLDP TLVs. There are two main versions of DCBx: IEEE DCBx and CEE DCBx. AOS-CX switches support the IEEE DCBx version which uses an OUI of 0x0080c2. DCBx supports VSX synchronization. For more information about enabling VSX synchronization, see the Virtual Switching Extension (VSX) Guide for your switch and software version. DCBx LLDP TLVs supported by Arub a OS-CX: • PFC (Priority Flow Control) TLV with subtype 0x0b. ◦ Advertises priorities that are configured in the switch as lossy/lossless. ◦ PFC TLVs are symmetrical which means they have to match between peers. ◦ If a peer PFC priority configuration does not match switch configuration, a misconfiguration error is displayed by the command show dcbx interface. • ETS (Enhanced Transmission Selection) configuration TLV with subtype 0x09.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the lldp dcbx command do?

The lldp dcbx command globally enables DCBx. The no form of this command disables DCBx advertisement. Enables DCBx on an interface. Default is enabled. DCBx must be enabled globally for this configuration to take effect. The no form of this command disables DCBx on an interface. Configures application to priority map that gets advertised in DCBx application priority messages. This tells the DCBx peer to send the application traffic with the configured priority so that the traffic is treated as lossless. Multiple applications can be configured in this manner. PFC lossless priority configured on the switch should be the same as this priority. NOTE: Priority Flow Control (PFC) commands are only supported on the 8325 and 8360. The no form of this command removes the existing configuration. Syntax: lldp dcbx.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the lldp dcbx command?
The syntax of the lldp dcbx command is: lldp dcbx.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show dcbx interface command do?

The show dcbx interface command shows the current DCBx status and the configuration of PFC, ETS, and application priority applied on the interface and the status of the TLVs received from the peer. Syntax: show dcbx interface <IFNAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show dcbx interface command?
The syntax of the show dcbx interface command is: show dcbx interface <IFNAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the guide say about Zero Touch Provisioning?

Zero Touch Provisioning: (ZTP) enables the auto-configuration of factory default switches without a network administrator onsite. When a switch is booted from its factory default configuration, ZTP autoprovisions the switch by automatically downloading and installing a firmware file, a configuration file, or both. With ZTP, even a nontechnical user (for example: a store manager in a retail chain or a teacher in a school) can deploy devices at a site. ZTP support The switch supports standards-based Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations as follows: • The switch must be running the factory default configuration. • The switch can connect to the DHCP server from the OOBM management port. • ZTP operations are supported over IPv4 connections only. IPv6 connections are not supported for ZTP operations. • You must configure the DHCP server to provide a standards-based ZTP server solution. Options and features that are specific to Network Management Solution (NMS) tools, such as AirWave, are not supported.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ztp information command do?
The show ztp information command shows information about Zero Touch Provisioning (ZTP) operations performed on the switch. Syntax: show ztp information.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ztp information command?
The syntax of the show ztp information command is: show ztp information.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ztp force-provision command do?
The ztp force-provision command starts on-demand ZTP. Syntax: ztp force-provision.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ztp force-provision command?
The syntax of the ztp force-provision command is: ztp force-provision.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bluetooth disable command do?

The bluetooth disable command disables the Bluetooth feature on the switch. The Bluetooth feature includes both Bluetooth Classic and Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE). Bluetooth is enabled by default. The no form of this command enables the Bluetooth feature on the switch. This command enables the Bluetooth feature on the switch. The Bluetooth feature includes both Bluetooth Classic and Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE). Default: Bluetooth is enabled by default. Syntax: bluetooth disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bluetooth disable command?
The syntax of the bluetooth disable command is: bluetooth disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the hostname <HOSTNAME> command do?

The hostname <HOSTNAME> command sets the host name of the switch. The no form of this command sets the host name to the default value, which is switch. Sets the state of the locator LED. Traces the specified IPv4 source and group addresses. Syntax: hostname <HOSTNAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the hostname <HOSTNAME> command?
The syntax of the hostname <HOSTNAME> command is: hostname <HOSTNAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show bluetooth command do?
The show bluetooth command shows general status information about the Bluetooth wireless management feature on the switch. Syntax: show bluetooth.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show usb file-system command do?

The show usb file-system command shows directory listings for a mounted USB device. When entered without the <PATH> parameter the top level directory tree is shown. Syntax: show usb file-system [<PATH>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show usb file-system command?
The syntax of the show usb file-system command is: show usb file-system [<PATH>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the system resource-utilization poll-interval <SECONDS> command do?

The system resource-utilization poll-interval <SECONDS> command configures the polling interval for system resource information collection and recording such as CPU and memory usage. Shows CPU utilization information. Shows memory utilization information. Enables the USB ports on the switch. This setting is persistent across switch reboots and management module failovers. Both active and standby management modules are affected by this setting. The no form of this command disables the USB ports. Syntax: system resource-utilization poll-interval <SECONDS>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the system resource-utilization poll-interval <SECONDS> command?
The syntax of the system resource-utilization poll-interval <SECONDS> command is: system resource-utilization poll-interval <SECONDS>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the usb {mount | unmount} command do?
The usb {mount | unmount} command enables or disables the inserted USB drive. Syntax: usb {mount | unmount}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the usb {mount | unmount} command?
The syntax of the usb {mount | unmount} command is: usb {mount | unmount}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the redundancy switchover command do?

The redundancy switchover command causes the switch to immediately switch over to the Standby Management Module. This command must be executed from the Active Management Module and will fail if the Standby Management Module is in a failed state or not present. Syntax: redundancy switchover.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the redundancy switchover command?
The syntax of the redundancy switchover command is: redundancy switchover.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is BFD?

BFD: NOTE: The BFD feature and thus this entire chapter is not applicable to the 6200 Switch Series. Bidirectional Forwarding Detection (BFD) provides a general-purpose, standard, medium- and protocol- independent fast failure detection mechanism. It can detect and monitor the connectivity of links in IP to detect communication failures quickly. BFD operates independently of media, data protocols, and routing protocols. BFD establishes a session between two network devices to detect failures on the bidirectional forwarding paths between the devices and provide services for upper-layer protocols. BFD provides no neighbor discovery mechanism. Protocols that BFD services notify BFD of devices to which it needs to establish sessions. After a session is established, if no BFD control packet is received from the peer within the negotiated BFD interval, BFD notifies a failure to the protocol, which then takes appropriate measures. BFD operates in two modes: • Asynchronous mode: In this mode, an operating device periodically sends BFD control packets to another device.

section: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 static route

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD for an IPv4 static route?
To configure BFD for an IPv4 static route, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on an IPv4 static route with the command ip route bfd.

task: Configuring BFD for an IPv4 static route

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD For OSPFv2?

To configure BFD For OSPFv2, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on all OSPF interfaces with the command bfd all-interfaces, or enable BFD on a specific. Commands shown in the procedure include: interface with the command ip ospf bfd..
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD For OSPFv3?

To configure BFD For OSPFv3, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on all OSPF interfaces with the command bfd all-interfaces, or enable BFD on a specific. Commands shown in the procedure include: interface with the command IPv6 ospfv3 bfd..
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure BFD for VRRP?
To configure BFD for VRRP, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable BFD support with the command bfd 2. Enable BFD on a VRRP interface with the command bfd <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd all-interfaces command do?

The bfd all-interfaces command enables BFD on all OSPFv2 or OSPFv3 interfaces. The no form of this command disables BFD on all OSPFv2/OSPFv3 or PIM IPv4/IPv6 interfaces, excluding those on which BFD was enabled at the interface level with the commands ip ospf bfd and IPv6 ospfv3 bfd. Syntax: bfd all-interfaces.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command?
The syntax of the bfd all-interfaces command is: bfd all-interfaces.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd detect-multiplier command do?
The bfd detect-multiplier command is documented in this guide. Syntax: bfd detect-multiplier <MULTIPLIER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command?
The syntax of the bfd detect-multiplier command is: bfd detect-multiplier <MULTIPLIER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd disable command do?
The bfd disable command disables BFD on the switch, but retains all configuration settings. Syntax: bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd disable command?
The syntax of the bfd disable command is: bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd enable command do?
The bfd enable command enables or disables BFD for HSC feature. Syntax: bfd enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd enable command?
The syntax of the bfd enable command is: bfd enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd echo disable command do?
The bfd echo disable command is documented in this guide. Syntax: bfd echo disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd echo disable command?
The syntax of the bfd echo disable command is: bfd echo disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd echo-src-ip-address command do?

The bfd echo-src-ip-address command sets the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets. This address is used in all echo sessions. NOTE: The source IP address must not be on the same network segment as any switch interface, otherwise a large number of ICMP redirect packets may be sent by the remote device, causing network congestion. The no form of this command removes the source IPv4 address for BFD echo packets, which causes the switch to stop sending echo packets. When a valid value is set, all sessions with a peer that is capable of receiving echo packets, will start transmitting echo packets. BFD control sessions continue to run concurrently with echo packets. 30 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command?
The syntax of the bfd echo-src-ip-address command is: bfd echo-src-ip-address <IPv4-ADDR>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-echo-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD echo packets. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD echo packets to the default value of 500 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command?
The syntax of the bfd min-echo-receive-interval command is: bfd min-echo-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-receive-interval command do?

The bfd min-receive-interval command sets the minimum time interval between received BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between received BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 milliseconds. Syntax: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command?
The syntax of the bfd min-receive-interval command is: bfd min-receive-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bfd min-transmit-interval command do?

The bfd min-transmit-interval command sets the minimum time interval between transmitted BFD control packets on an interface. The no form of this command sets the minimum interval between transmitted BFD control packets to the default value of 3000 ms. Syntax: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command?
The syntax of the bfd min-transmit-interval command is: bfd min-transmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear bfd statistics command do?
The clear bfd statistics command clears statistics for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: clear bfd statistics [session <ID>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear bfd statistics command?
The syntax of the clear bfd statistics command is: clear bfd statistics [session <ID>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf bfd disable command do?
The ip ospf bfd disable command disables BFD for OSPFv2 on the current interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command?
The syntax of the ip ospf bfd disable command is: ip ospf bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route bfd command do?

The ip route bfd command enables or disables BFD on the specified static route. To disable BFD, issue the command without the bfd option. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route bfd command?
The syntax of the ip route bfd command is: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR> | <INTERFACE>] [bfd]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command disables BFD on the current OSPFv3 interface. This overrides the global settings defined with the command bfd all-interfaces. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable command is: IPv6 ospfv3 bfd disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor fall-over bfd command do?

The neighbor fall-over bfd command enables BGP to register with BFD to receive fast peering session deactivation messages from BFD. The no form of this command disables BGP for BFD. NOTE: Currently, BFD is supported only with IPv4 neighbors. Support to enable BFD with IPv6 neighbors will be added in a future release. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over bfd.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor fall-over bfd command?
The syntax of the neighbor fall-over bfd command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over bfd.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show bfd command do?
The show bfd command shows information for all BFD sessions or for a specific BFD session. Syntax: show bfd [session <ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show bfd command?
The syntax of the show bfd command is: show bfd [session <ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show hsc command do?
The show hsc command displays connection information for the remote controller. Syntax: show hsc.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show hsc command?
The syntax of the show hsc command is: show hsc.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command do?

The clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command removes the protection switching and triggers reversion both in revertive and non-revertive operation. This command will not change the configured revertive operation mode. Syntax: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command?
The syntax of the clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id> command is: clear erps ring <ringid> instance <id>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear erps statistics command do?
The clear erps statistics command this command clears the ERPS statistics for a ring or a ring instance. Syntax: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear erps statistics command?
The syntax of the clear erps statistics command is: clear erps statistics [ring <id>] [instance <id>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring command do?
The erps ring command this command creates an ERPS ring with a given ID. The no form of this command removes all the configurations of the ring, including instances. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command do?

The erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command this command configures the ERPS ring member port. An L2 interface in the switch is associated to one of the two member ports of an ERPS ring. In case of an interconnection node, only port0 is applicable for the sub-ring. The no form of this command removes the association of the ring port to the L2 interface on the switch. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> <port0 port1> interface command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> description command do?

The erps ring <ringid> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> description command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> description command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command guard timer is used in nodes recovering from a local failure to avoid loops due to earlier Signal Fail (SF) messages that may be in the ring. The configuration specifies the guard timer duration in units of 10 ms. The timer period must be greater than the maximum expected forwarding delay in which an R-APS message traverses the entire ring. The default value is 50. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the guard interval and sets it to the default value of 50. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> guard-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command specifies hold-off interval in units of 100 ms. If specified, a defect is not reported immediately. Instead, the hold-off timer is started. On expiration of the timer, if the defect still exists, it is reported to protection switching. The default value for hold-off timer is 0. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the hold-off interval and sets it to the default value of 0. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> hold-off-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance command on a common ERPS network, a physical ring can be configured with a single ERPS ring, and only one blocked port can be specified in the ring. When the ERPS ring is in normal state, the blocked port prohibits all service packets from passing through. As a result, all service data is transmitted through one path over the ERPS ring, and the other link on the blocked port becomes idle, leading to ineffective use of bandwidth. To improve link use efficiency, logical rings can be configured in the same physical ring in the ERPS multi- instance. A port may have different roles in different ERPS rings and different ERPS rings use different control VLANs. An ERPS ring must be configured with an ERP instance, and each ERP instance specifies a range of VLANs. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command this command adds a control-channel VLAN to a ring instance. In an ERPS ring, the control VLANshould be used only to forward RAPS PDUs and not service packets. All the devices in an ERPS ring instance must be configured with the same control VLAN, and different ERPS ring instances must use different control VLANs. The no form of this command removes the control-channel VLAN of the ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> control-vlan command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command this command adds descriptive information to help administrators and operators understand the purpose of a ring instance. 1-64 printable ASCII characters are allowed. The no form of this command removes the ring instance description. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> description command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command this configuration enables protection switching on the given instance of the ring. It is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables protection switching on the given instance of the ring. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> enable command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command this command specifies the set of VLANs that are protected by this ring instance. The no form of this command removes a set of VLANs that are protected by this ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protected-vlans command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command blocks a specific ring interface in one of the two following ways: • Force: The switch blocks a specific ring interface regardless of the protection switching state of the ring instance. • Manual: The switch blocks a specific ring interface if no other protection switch event is active on the ring instance. NOTE: The user can verify whether the protection-switch is successful by verifying the status of instance and port state over which this command is executed. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch {{manual|force} <port0>|<port1>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch command is: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> protection-switch {{manual|force} <port0>|<port1>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command configures the default revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In revertive operation, after the conditions causing protection switching are cleared, traffic channels are restored to the recovered link blocking the RPL. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. The no form of this command configures non-revertive mode of operation for an ERPS ring. In non-revertive operation, the traffic channels continue to use the RPL, if it has not failed, after conditions causing protection switching are cleared. This configuration is meaningful only on the RPL node. Syntax: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive command is: erps ring <ringid> instance <id> revertive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching on/off the RPL link. This command specifies the role of the node as owner or neighbor. The no form of this command removes the configuration of the node role from the instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> role command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command do?

The erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command in ERPS, there is a central node called RPL Owner Node which blocks one of the ports to ensure that there is no loop formed for the Ethernet traffic. The link blocked by the RPL owner node is called the Ring Protection Link or RPL. The node at the other end of the RPL is known as RPL Neighbor Node. It uses R-APS control messages to coordinate the activities of switching the RPL link on and off. This command specifies which of the ERPS ring ports is the RPL. The no form of this command removes the RPL port configuration from the ERPS ring instance. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> instance <id> rpl command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command do?

The erps ring <ringid> meg-level command the R-APS messages transmitted by ERPS take the form of OAM PDUs as defined in G.8013. Each OAM PDU is transmitted at a specified level known as the Maintenance Entity Group (MEG) level. This command configures the level with which the ERPS packets must be transmitted. The no form of this command removes the configured MEG level and sets it to the default value of 7. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> meg-level command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command do?

The erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command this command associates a sub-ring to a parent-ring and is required for the sub-ring to notify the parent- ring on change in topology. The no form of this command removes the parent ring identifier. 56 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> parent-ring command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command do?

The erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command this command is to configure a sub-ring. If not specified, the ring is a major-ring. The no form of this command removes the sub-ring configuration of the ring and configures it to be a major-ring. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> sub-ring command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command do?

The erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command this command is to configure a sub-ring interconnection node to pass a topology change notification to the ring instance for the parent ring whenever the topology of the sub-ring changes. The parent ring instance performs a Forwarding Database (FDB) flush and sends a protocol message to ensure that other nodes on the parent ring also perform an FDB flush. The no form of this command disables topology change notifications. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> tcn-propogation command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command specifies the R-APS periodic transmission interval in units of seconds. Default is 5 seconds. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the transmission interval and sets it to the default value of 5 seconds. 58 AOS-CX 10.06 High Availability Guide. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> transmission-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command do?

The erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command the RPL owner node uses a delay timer before initiating an RPL block in case of both revertive mode of operation or before reverting to idle state after clearing operator commands (FS, MS). The Wait to Restore (WTR) timer can be configured in 1-minute increments up to 12 minutes. The default value is 5 minutes. When recovering from an SF, the delay timer must be long enough to allow the recovering network to become stable. In the default revertive mode of operation, the WTR timer is used to prevent frequent operation of protection switching due to intermittent SF defects. The no form of this command removes the configured value of the wtr-interval and sets it to the default value of 5 minutes. Syntax: erps ring <ringid>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command?
The syntax of the erps ring <ringid> wtr-interval command is: erps ring <ringid>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps statistics command do?

The show erps statistics command this command displays ERPS statistics. The statistics can be displayed for the ring, the instance, or the instance ports. Syntax: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps statistics command?
The syntax of the show erps statistics command is: show erps statistics [ring <ringid>] [instance <id> [port0|port1]]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps status command do?

The show erps status command this command displays detailed information about a specific ring or all instances of a ring. The ring instance may be in one of the following states: • • Idle: The ring instance is operational. Initializing: The ring instance is not operational. • Protection: Protection switching has been triggered by a local or remote link failure. • Pending: Pending clearance of a previous protection switch. • Down: Ring instance is not active. • Manual-switch: Manual protection switching triggered by Admin-down. • Force-switch: Forced protection switching triggered by admin. A ring instance has the following reasons for "down" state: . Syntax: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps status command?
The syntax of the show erps status command is: show erps status [ring <ringid>] [instance <id>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show erps summary command do?
The show erps summary command this command displays a summary of the ERPS configuration and state for the ERPS ring instances. Syntax: show erps summary.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show erps summary command?
The syntax of the show erps summary command is: show erps summary.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you accessing the AOS-CX Web UI?

To accessing the AOS-CX Web UI, follow the documented procedure: 1. Start a supported web browser and enter the IP address of the management port in the address bar. Use 2. Optionally a pre- and post-login message may be displayed. The message can be customized or disabled 3. At the login page, enter your user name and password credentials, then click Login 4. After you log in, the main Web UI page is displayed.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you troubleshoot Web UI access issues?

To troubleshoot Web UI access issues, follow the documented procedure: 1. Log out from one of the existing sessions 2. If the session cookie is lost and it is not possible to log out of the session, then wait for the session idle.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you add and deleting a VLAN?
To add and deleting a VLAN, follow the documented procedure: 1. To add a VLAN: 2. To delete a VLAN: .
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route vrf command do?
The IPv6 route vrf command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole] vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route vrf command?
The syntax of the IPv6 route vrf command is: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<PREFIX> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole] vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vrf attach command do?

The vrf attach command attaches the interface to the VRF with the name <VRF-NAME>. The command can be entered in several different command contexts. CAUTION: The no form of the command detaches the interface from the named VRF and will remove all configurations from the interface and attach the interface to the default VRF. A warning message is displayed that prompts you whether to proceed: All Layer 3 configurations associated with the VRF will be deleted. Continue (y/n)? . Syntax: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vrf attach command?
The syntax of the vrf attach command is: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 address command do?
The IPv6 address command sets the IPv6 address for a loopback interface. Syntax: IPv6 address <IPv6-ADDR/MASK>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the vrf attach <VRF-NAME> command do?

The vrf attach <VRF-NAME> command attaches a non-default VRF to a loopback. The no form of this command deletes a non-default VRF from a loopback and reattaches the default VRF. Syntax: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the vrf attach <VRF-NAME> command?
The syntax of the vrf attach <VRF-NAME> command is: vrf attach <VRF-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show interface loopback command do?
The show interface loopback command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show interface loopback [brief | instance <ID>] [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show interface loopback command?
The syntax of the show interface loopback command is: show interface loopback [brief | instance <ID>] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route command do?

The ip route command adds an IPv4 static route on the default VRF. The no form of this command deletes a IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route command?
The syntax of the ip route command is: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route distance command do?

The ip route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route distance command?
The syntax of the ip route distance command is: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<NETMASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route tag command do?

The ip route tag command configures tag for IPv4 static route. The no form of this command deletes tag for IPv4 static route. Syntax: ip route <A. B. C. D/M> [A. B. C. D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route tag command?
The syntax of the ip route tag command is: ip route <A. B. C. D/M> [A. B. C. D | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route vrf command do?
The ip route vrf command is documented in this guide. Syntax: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole] vrf <VRF-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route vrf command?
The syntax of the ip route vrf command is: ip route <DEST-IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole] vrf <VRF-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route command do?
The IPv6 route command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route command?
The syntax of the IPv6 route command is: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>|reject|blackhole]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route distance command do?

The IPv6 route distance command configures the administrative distance for the IPv6 static route The no form of this command deletes the static route. Syntax: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route distance command?
The syntax of the IPv6 route distance command is: IPv6 route <DEST-IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [<NEXT-HOP-IP-ADDR>|<INTERFACE>] distance <VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route tag command do?
The IPv6 route tag command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 route <X: X: : X: X/M> [X: X: : X: X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route tag command?
The syntax of the IPv6 route tag command is: IPv6 route <X: X: : X: X/M> [X: X: : X: X | INTERFACE] tag <1-4294967295>

section: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure OSPF on the routing switch?

To configure OSPF on the routing switch, follow the documented procedure: 1. Create the OSPF instance and enter the OSPF router configuration context using the following command 2. Configure a global router ID using the following command. For command details, see router-id. Commands shown in the procedure include: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]; router-id <ROUTER-ADDRESS>.

task: Configuring OSPF on the routing switch

section: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure external route redistribution and control?

To configure external route redistribution and control, follow the documented procedure: 1. Enable route redistribution using the command redistribute 2. Optionally, modify the default metric for redistribution using the following command. For command 3. Optionally, set the cost of default-summary LSAs using the following command. For command details, 4. Optionally, set the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer this router.

task: Configuring external route redistribution and control

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, how do you configure OSPF interface authentication?

To configure OSPF interface authentication, follow the documented procedure: 1. For simple-text authentication, set the password using the following command. For command details, see 2. For MD5 authentication, set the password using the following command. For command details, see ip. Commands shown in the procedure include: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}; ip ospf authentication-key.; ip ospf authentication-key <PASSWORD>; ip ospf message-digest-key {Key ID} md5 {ciphertext | plaintext} <KEY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command do?

The area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command sets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas. The no form of this command resets the cost of the default route announced to NSSA or stub areas, to the default value of 1. Syntax: area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command?
The syntax of the area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST> command is: area <AERA-ID> default-metric <COST>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command creates the NSSA area (Not So Stubby Area) with <AREA-ID> if not present. If area is present and not NSSA area, this command changes the area type to NSSA area. If no-summary is used, area type will be NSSA No- Summary. The no form of this command unsets the area type as NSSA. That is, the configured area will be changed to default normal area. The no area <AREA-ID> nssa no-summary command enables sending inter-area routes into NSSA, but will not unset the area as NSSA. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary] command is: area <AREA-ID> nssa [no-summary]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command summarizes the routes with the matching address or masks. This command only works for border routers. The no form of this command removes route summarization for the configured IPv4 prefix address on the ABR. When using the no form of the command with the no-advertise option, enables advertising this range to other areas. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise] command is: area <AREA-ID> range <IP-PREFIX> type {inter-area | nssa} [no-advertise]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary] command do?

The area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary] command creates the stub area with <AREA-ID> if not present. If the area is already present and it is not a normal stub area, then this command changes the stub area type to normal. If the no-summary parameter is used, area type will be stub No-Summary. The no form of this command unsets the area as a stub type. That is, the configured area will be changed to a default normal area. The no area <AREA-ID> stub no_summary command enables sending inter-area routes into the stub area, but will not unset the area as stub. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary] command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary] command is: area <AREA-ID> stub [no-summary]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command creates an OSPF virtual link with a remote ABR and enters the vlink context. The no form of this command deletes an OSPF virtual link with the specified router ID of the remote ABR. If no <ROUTER-ID> is specified, the no form of the command sets the virtual link to the default settings. Sets the OSPF virtual-link authentication type that will be used for authentication with the remote ABR. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID> command is: area <AREA-ID> virtual-link <ROUTER-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD> command do?

The authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD> command sets the OSPF virtual-link authentication password that is used for simple-text authentication. If the password is given in ciphertext, it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. The no form of this command deletes the virtual-link authentication password that is used for simple-text authentication. Syntax: authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD> command?
The syntax of the authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD> command is: authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear ip ospf statistics command do?
The clear ip ospf statistics command clear the OSPF event statistics. Syntax: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear ip ospf statistics command?
The syntax of the clear ip ospf statistics command is: clear ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the dead-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The dead-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet comes in for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the dead interval to default for virtual links. The default value is 40 seconds (generally four times the hello packet interval). NOTE: For proper operation, set the dead interval must be longer than the hello interval. Configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors if it is present in the routing table. The no form of this command disables advertisement of the default route. Syntax: dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the dead-interval <INTERVAL> command?
The syntax of the dead-interval <INTERVAL> command is: dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-information originate always command do?

The default-information originate always command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors, regardless if it is present in the routing table or not. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Syntax: default-information originate always.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-information originate always command?
The syntax of the default-information originate always command is: default-information originate always.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the disable command do?

The disable command sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPF. The no form of this command sets the default metric to be used for redistributed routes into OSPF to the default of 25. Disables the OSPF process. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPF configurations. Syntax: disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the disable command?
The syntax of the disable command is: disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the distance <DISTANCE> command do?

The distance <DISTANCE> command defines an administrative distance for OSPF. Administrative distance is used as a criteria to select the best route when multiple routes are present from different routing protocols. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Enables the OSPF process, if disabled. By default the OSPF process is enabled. Syntax: distance <DISTANCE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the distance <DISTANCE> command?
The syntax of the distance <DISTANCE> command is: distance <DISTANCE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command configures graceful restart parameters for OSPF. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default interval of 120 seconds or disables the helper mode, depending on the parameter specified. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command?
The syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper} command is: graceful-restart {restart-interval <SECONDS> | helper}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf area command do?

The ip ospf area command runs the OSPF protocol on the interface with the configured IPv4 address for the area specified. The interfaces which have an IP address configured in this network or in a subset of this network, will participate in the OSPF protocol. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf area command?
The syntax of the ip ospf area command is: ip ospf <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication command do?

The ip ospf authentication command sets the authentication type that will be used for authentication with the neighbor router. The no form of this command deletes the authentication type used for a particular authentication with the neighbor router and sets to null authentication. Syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication command?
The syntax of the ip ospf authentication command is: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}.

syntax: ip ospf authentication {message-digest | simple-text | null | keychain}

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf authentication-key command do?

The ip ospf authentication-key command sets the authentication password used for simple-text authentication. If the password is given in ciphertext it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. The no form of this command deletes the authentication password used for simple-text authentication. Syntax: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf authentication-key command?
The syntax of the ip ospf authentication-key command is: ip ospf authentication-key {ciphertext | plaintext} <PASSWORD>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf cost command do?

The ip ospf cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default cost 1. Syntax: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf cost command?
The syntax of the ip ospf cost command is: ip ospf cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf dead-interval command do?
The ip ospf dead-interval command sets the interval after which a neighbor is declared dead if no hello packet is received on the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command?
The syntax of the ip ospf dead-interval command is: ip ospf dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf hello-interval command do?

The ip ospf hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval OSPF hello packets to the default of 10 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf hello-interval command?
The syntax of the ip ospf hello-interval command is: ip ospf hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf keychain command do?

The ip ospf keychain command sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf keychain command?
The syntax of the ip ospf keychain command is: ip ospf keychain <KEYCHAIN-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf network command do?

The ip ospf network command configures the OSPF network type for the interface. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default which is broadcast network. Syntax: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf network command?
The syntax of the ip ospf network command is: ip ospf network {broadcast | point-to-point}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf passive command do?

The ip ospf passive command configures the interface as an OSPF passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in OSPF but does not send or receive packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: ip ospf passive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf passive command?
The syntax of the ip ospf passive command is: ip ospf passive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf priority command do?

The ip ospf priority command sets the OSPF priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chances for it to become the designated router. Setting a priority of zero makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPF priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf priority command?
The syntax of the ip ospf priority command is: ip ospf priority <PRIORITY-VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf retransmit-interval command do?

The ip ospf retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for the OSPF interface. Syntax: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf retransmit-interval command?
The syntax of the ip ospf retransmit-interval command is: ip ospf retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip ospf transit-delay command do?

The ip ospf transit-delay command sets the time delay in link state transmission for the OSPF interface. The no form of this command sets the delay in link state transmission to the default of 1 second for the OSPF interface. Sets the key chain for md5 authentication. A key chain configures rotating keys for packet authenticating, reducing the risk of keys being compromised. The no form of this command deletes the key chain used for md5 authentication. Syntax: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip ospf transit-delay command?
The syntax of the ip ospf transit-delay command is: ip ospf transit-delay <DELAY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer this router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup for the time mentioned in seconds or for a default value of 600 seconds. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command?
The syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]] command is: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup [<ADVERT-TIME>]]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE> command do?

The maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE> command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPF can support. The no form of this command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPF can support to the default value of 4. Syntax: maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE> command?
The syntax of the maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE> command is: maximum-paths <MAX-VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the passive-interface default command do?

The passive-interface default command sets the message digest password that is used for message digest authentication. If the md5 key is given in ciphertext, it will be decrypted and applied to the protocol. The no form of this command deletes the message-digest password that is used for message digest authentication Configures all OSPF interfaces as passive. The no form of this command sets all OSPF interfaces as active. Syntax: passive-interface default.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the passive-interface default command?
The syntax of the passive-interface default command is: passive-interface default.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command do?

The redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command redistributes routes originating from other protocols, or from another OSPF process, to the current OSPF process. The no form of this command disables redistribution of routes to the current OSPF process. Syntax: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command?

The syntax of the redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command is: redistribute {bgp | connected | static | rip | ospf <PROCESS-ID>} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command do?

The reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPFv2. If the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set. then the cost of all the OSPFv2 interfaces is recalculated based on the reference bandwidth and link speed of the interface. For VLAN interfaces the link speed value is taken as 1 Gbps (if the OSPFv2 interface cost is not explicitly set). The no form of this command sets the reference bandwidth for OSPF to the default of 100000 Mbps. Syntax: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command?
The syntax of the reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH> command is: reference-bandwidth <BANDWIDTH>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default of 5 seconds for virtual links. Enables OSPF compatibility with RFC1583 (backward compatibility). If RFC1583 compatibility is enabled, then the route cost calculation follows a different method. The no form of this command disables OSPF compatibility with RFC1583 (backward compatibility). By default the RFC1583 compatibility is disabled. Syntax: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command?
The syntax of the retransmit-interval <INTERVAL> command is: retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospf command do?

The router ospf command creates an OSPF process (if not created already) on a VRF, and switches to the OSPF router instance context. Up to eight OSPF processes are supported per VRF. The no form of this command removes the OSPF instance. Syntax: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospf command?
The syntax of the router ospf command is: router ospf <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router-id command do?

The router-id command sets an ID for the router in an IPv4 address format. The no form of this command unconfigures the router-id for the instance and sets the router-id to the default as follows: the router-id is selected dynamically as equal to the highest loopback address on the router, or the highest active interface if there are no loopback addresses. If no IP address is configured on any interfaces on the router, OSPF will not form an adjacency. Syntax: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router-id command?
The syntax of the router-id command is: router-id <ROUTER-ADDR>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf interface command do?
The show ip ospf interface command displays general OSPF, area, state, and configuration information. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf interface command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf interface command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<interface-name>] [brief]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf lsdb command do?
The show ip ospf lsdb command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf lsdb command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf lsdb command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] lsdb [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf neighbors command do?
The show ip ospf neighbors command displays information about OSPF neighbors. 102. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf neighbors command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf neighbors command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf routes command do?
The show ip ospf routes command is documented in this guide. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf routes command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf routes command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] routes.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf statistics command do?
The show ip ospf statistics command displays OSPF statistics. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf statistics command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf statistics command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf statistics interface command do?
The show ip ospf statistics interface command displays OSPF statistics for the OSPF-enabled interfaces. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf statistics interface command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf statistics interface command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show ip ospf virtual-links command do?

The show ip ospf virtual-links command displays the current state and parameters of the OSPF virtual links. Syntax: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] virtual-links [brief] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip ospf virtual-links command?
The syntax of the show ip ospf virtual-links command is: show ip ospf [<PROCESS-ID>] virtual-links [brief] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
The syntax of the summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command is: summary-address <IPv4-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command do?

The timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command configures the minimum delay between receiving the same LSA from a peer. The same LSA is an LSA that contains the same LSA ID number, LSA type, and advertising router ID. If an instance of the same LSA arrives sooner before the delay expires, the LSA is dropped. Generally, the LSA arrival timer should be set to a value less than or equal to the start-time value for the command timers throttle lsa start on the neighbor. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Syntax: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command?
The syntax of the timers lsa-arrival <DELAY> command is: timers lsa-arrival <DELAY>

section: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command do?

The timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command configures the timers for LSA generation. The no form of this command sets the LSA timers to default values. Syntax: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>.

command: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>

The syntax of the timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME> command is: timers throttle lsa start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TIME> max-wait-time <WAIT-TIME>
