messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command do?

The timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command configures timers for SPF calculation. There are three timers: • start-time Is the initial delay before an SPF calculation is started. Default is 200 milliseconds. • hold-time Is the progressive backoff time to wait before next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 1000 milliseconds. If a route change event occurs during this period, the value doubles until it reaches the max-wait-time. • max-wait-time Is the maximum time to wait before the next scheduled SPF calculation. Default is 5000 milliseconds. This is used to limit the SPF hold timer and also defines the time to be considered for which the OSPF LSDB has to be stable, after which the SPF throttle mechanism is reset. The no form of this command sets all the configured non-default timers to default value. Syntax: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command?
The syntax of the timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME> command is: timers throttle spf start-time <START-TIME> hold-time <HOLD-TINME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the transit-delay <SECONDS> command do?

The transit-delay <SECONDS> command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second for virtual links. Syntax: transit-delay <SECONDS>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the transit-delay <SECONDS> command?
The syntax of the transit-delay <SECONDS> command is: transit-delay <SECONDS>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> command creates a normal area with <AREA-ID> set if not present. If area is present and is not the normal area, this command changes the area type to normal area. The no form of this command deletes the area with the <AREA-ID> specified. The area can be of any type (stub, stub no-summary, and default normal area). Syntax: area <AREA-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> command is: area <AREA-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces which have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?

The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command is: area <AREA-ID> authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPsec ESP with authentication/encryption algorithm type and key for the specified area. OSPFv3 interfaces with IPsec configured at interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP configuration. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP from the specified area. NOTE: IPsec is not supported for 6in6 tunnel interfaces. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command is: area <AREA-ID> encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC> command do?

The area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC> command sets the cost of default-summary LSAs announced to the stub/nssa areas. The no form of this command resets the cost of the default-summary LSAs announced to stub/nssa areas to the default of 1. Syntax: area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC> command?
The syntax of the area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC> command is: area <AREA-ID> default-metric <METRIC>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?
The clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command clears the OSPFv3 event statistics. Syntax: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
The syntax of the clear IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command is: clear IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics [interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command do?

The default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command sets the default metric for redistributed routes in the OSPFv3. The no form of this command sets the default metric to be used for redistributed routes into OSPFv3 to the default of 25. Disables the OSPFv3 process. By default OSPFv3 process is enabled. IMPORTANT: This command does not remove the OSPFv3 configurations. Syntax: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command?
The syntax of the default-metric <METRIC-VALUE> command is: default-metric <METRIC-VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command do?

The encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command configures IPSec ESP for Vlink. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP for Vlink from the specified area. Syntax: encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command?
The syntax of the encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY> command is: encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the default-information originate command do?

The default-information originate command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors if it is present in the routing table. The no form of this command disables advertisement of the default route. Syntax: default-information originate.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the default-information originate command?
The syntax of the default-information originate command is: default-information originate.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command do?

The graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command configures OSPF to advertise the default route (0.0.0.0/0) to its neighbors, regardless if it is present in the routing table or not. The no form of this command sets the OSPF administrative distance to the default of 110. Configures graceful restart for OSPFv3. By default graceful restart is enabled on the OSPFv3 router. The no form of this command sets the restart interval to the default of 120 seconds or disables helper mode depending on the specified parameters. Syntax: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command?
The syntax of the graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check} command is: graceful-restart {restart-interval <INTERVAL> | helper | strict-lsa-check}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the hello-interval <INTERVAL> command do?

The hello-interval <INTERVAL> command sets the time interval between OSPF hello packets for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the hello interval to the default value of 10 seconds for virtual links. Syntax: hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the hello-interval <INTERVAL> command?
The syntax of the hello-interval <INTERVAL> command is: hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 area command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 area command runs the OSPFv3 protocol on the interface for the area specified. To move an interface to a new area, unmap the existing area and then associate a new area with the interface. The no form of this command disables OSPF on the interface and removes the interface from the area. Interfaces which have an IP address configured on the network or in a subset of the network, stop participating in the OSPF protocol. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 area command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 area command is: IPv6 ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> area <AREA-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null command is: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication null.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command configures IPSec AH authentication. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec. The no form of this command removes IPsec AH authentication for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec command is: IPv6 ospfv3 authentication ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 cost command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface. The interface cost is used as a parameter to calculate the best routes. The no form of this command sets the cost (metric) associated with a particular interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 cost command is: IPv6 ospfv3 cost <INTERFACE-COST>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command is documented in this guide. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval command is: IPv6 ospfv3 dead-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command configures IPsec ESP. OSPFv3 interfaces that have IPsec configured at the interface context will not use area level IPsec ESP. The no form of this command removes IPsec ESP for the specified area. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec command is: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption ipsec spi <SPI-INDEX> <AUTH-TYPE> <KEY-TYPE> <AUTH-KEY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command do?
The IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command configures NULL ESP on an interface which disables IPsec ESP. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null command is: IPv6 ospfv3 encryption null.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time interval between OSPFv3 hello packets to the default for the OSPFv3 interface of 10 seconds. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval command is: IPv6 ospfv3 hello-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 network command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 network command configures the network type for the interface. By default the network type is broadcast network. The no form of this command sets the network type for the interface to the system default of broadcast network. 150. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 network command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 network command is: IPv6 ospfv3 network {broadcast|point-to-point}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 passive command configures the interface as an OSPFv3 passive interface. With this setting, the interface participates in the OSPF, but does not send or receive OSPF packets on that interface. The no form of this command resets the interface as active. With this setting, the interface starts sending and receiving OSPF packets. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 passive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 passive command is: IPv6 ospfv3 passive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 priority command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface. The larger the numeric value of the priority, the higher the chance it will become the designated router. Setting a priority of 0 makes the router ineligible to become a designated router or back up designated router. The no form of this command sets the OSPFv3 priority for the interface to the default of 1. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 priority command is: IPv6 ospfv3 priority <number-value>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the time between retransmitting lost link state advertisements to the default 5 seconds. 152. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval command is: IPv6 ospfv3 retransmit-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command disables OSPFv3 on the interface. The interface state changes to Down. It does not remove the interface from the OSPF area. To remove the interface, use the command no ip ospf area. The no form of this command re-enables OSPFv3 on the interface. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown command is: IPv6 ospfv3 shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command do?

The IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for the OSPFv3 interface. The no form of this command sets the transit delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second. Sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support. The no form of this command sets the maximum number of ECMP routes that OSPFv3 can support to the default value of 4. Syntax: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command?
The syntax of the IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay command is: IPv6 ospfv3 transit-delay <DELAY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command do?

The max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command sets the protocol to advertise a maximum metric so that other routers do not prefer the router as an intermediate hop in their shortest path first (SPF) calculations. If the on-startup parameter is used, the router is configured to advertise a maximum metric at startup. That is, for the time specified in seconds, or the default value of 600 seconds. To disable advertisement of the maximum metric, use the no form of the command. The no form of this command advertises the normal cost metrics instead of advertising the maximized cost metric. This setting causes the router to be considered in traffic forwarding. Syntax: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command?
The syntax of the max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>] command is: max-metric router-lsa [on-startup <INTERVAL>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router ospfv3 command do?

The router ospfv3 command creates the OSPFv3 process (if not created already) and enters the router OSPFv3 instance context. Optionally if specified, you can specify a named VRF, or the default VRF if the <vrf-name> is not specified. Only one OSPFv3 process is allowed per VRF. Syntax: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router ospfv3 command?
The syntax of the router ospfv3 command is: router ospfv3 <PROCESS-ID> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command do?

The show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command shows the OSPFv3 routing table entries for Area Border Router (ABR) and Autonomous System Border Router (ASBR). Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 border-routers command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] border-routers [all-vrfs | vrf <VRF-NAME>] [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command shows information about OSPFv3 enabled interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 interface command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>] [brief]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command shows information about OSPFv3 neighbors. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 neighbors command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] neighbors [<NEIGHBOR-ID>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command shows the OSPFv3 routing table information. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 routes command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] routes [<PREFIX/LENGTH>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command shows OSPFv3 statistics. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command shows the OSPFv3 statistics for the OSPFv3-enabled interfaces. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 statistics interface command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] statistics interface [<INTERFACE-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show IPv6 ospfv3 virtual-links command do?
The show IPv6 ospfv3 virtual-links command displays the current state and parameters of the OSPFv3 virtual links. Syntax: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] virtual-links [brief].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 virtual-links command?
The syntax of the show IPv6 ospfv3 virtual-links command is: show IPv6 ospfv3 [<PROCESS-ID>] virtual-links [brief]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command do?

The summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command summarizes the external routes with the matching address and mask. When advertising this route, its metric is set to the lowest cost path from among the routes that were summarized. The no form of this command disables route summarization. NOTE: This command only works for an ASBR (Autonomous System Boundary Router). Syntax: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command?
The syntax of the summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>] command is: summary-address <IPv6-ADDR>/<MASK> [no-advertise | tag <TAG-VALUE>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the transit-delay <DELAY> command do?

The transit-delay <DELAY> command sets the time delay in Link state transmission for virtual links. The no form of this command sets the delay in Link state transmission to the default of 1 second for virtual links. Enables the notification of the events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations for OSPFv3. The no form of this command disables the notification of the events to be sent as traps to the SNMP management stations for OSPFv3. Syntax: transit-delay <DELAY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the transit-delay <DELAY> command?
The syntax of the transit-delay <DELAY> command is: transit-delay <DELAY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the address-family {IPv4 | IPv6} unicast command do?

The address-family {IPv4 | IPv6} unicast command specifies address family to use and changes to the configuration context for the specified family: • config-bgp-IPv4 for IPv4 • config-bgp-IPv6 for IPv6 • config-bgp-IPv4-uc for IPv4 unicast • config-bgp-IPv6-uc for IPv6 unicast The no form of this command removes the specified address family configuration. Syntax: address-family {IPv4 | IPv6} unicast.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the address-family {IPv4 | IPv6} unicast command?
The syntax of the address-family {IPv4 | IPv6} unicast command is: address-family {IPv4 | IPv6} unicast.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the aggregate-address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> [as-set] [summary-only] command do?

The aggregate-address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> [as-set] [summary-only] command creates an aggregate address entry in the BGP routing table. The no form of this command removes the specified aggregate address entry. Syntax: aggregate-address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> [as-set] [summary-only].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the aggregate-address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> [as-set] [summary-only] command?
The syntax of the aggregate-address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> [as-set] [summary-only] command is: aggregate-address <IP-ADDR>/<MASK> [as-set] [summary-only]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp always-compare-med command do?

The bgp always-compare-med command enables comparison of the Multi Exit Discriminator (MED) for paths from neighbors in different autonomous systems. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. The no form of this command sets comparison of MED to the default setting (disabled). Syntax: bgp always-compare-med.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp always-compare-med command?
The syntax of the bgp always-compare-med command is: bgp always-compare-med.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp asnotation dotted command do?

The bgp asnotation dotted command specifies that Autonomous System (AS) numbers greater than 65535 be shown in dotted integer format for all show commands, including running-configuration. The no form of this command restores the default format of non-dotted, simple integer. Specifies that all Autonomous System (AS) numbers be shown in dotted integer format for all show commands, including running-configuration. The no form of this command restores the default format of non-dotted, simple integer. Syntax: bgp asnotation dotted.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp asnotation dotted command?
The syntax of the bgp asnotation dotted command is: bgp asnotation dotted.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp bestpath as-path ignore command do?

The bgp bestpath as-path ignore command configures BGP to avoid considering the autonomous system (AS) path during best path route selection. By default, the AS-path is considered during BGP best path selection. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. The no form of this command restores default behavior which configures BGP to consider the AS-path during route selection. Configures Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) to treat two BGP routes as equal cost even if their AS-paths differ, as long as their AS-path lengths and other relevant attributes are the same. This allows routes with different AS-paths to be programmed into the forwarding table as equal cost multipath routes. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. Syntax: bgp bestpath as-path ignore.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp bestpath as-path ignore command?
The syntax of the bgp bestpath as-path ignore command is: bgp bestpath as-path ignore.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp bestpath compare-routerid command do?

The bgp bestpath compare-routerid command configures a BGP routing process to compare identical routes received from different external peers during the best path selection process and selects the route with the lowest router ID as the best path. Defaults to disabled. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions in the VRFs. The no form of this command returns the BGP routing process to the default operation. By default, BGP selects the route that was received first when two routes with identical attributes are received. Syntax: bgp bestpath compare-routerid.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp bestpath compare-routerid command?
The syntax of the bgp bestpath compare-routerid command is: bgp bestpath compare-routerid.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp bestpath med confed command do?

The bgp bestpath med confed command compares the identical routes received from the different confederation peers and selects the route with the lowest Multi Exit Discriminator (MED) value as the best path. This behavior is disabled by default. The no form of this command prevents the routing process from considering the MED value. NOTE: The selection of other attributes like as-multi-path relax and as-path ignore will not affect the behavior of this command within a confederation. Configures a BGP routing process to assign a value of infinity (max possible) to routes that are missing the Multi Exit Discriminator (MED) attribute. The path without a MED value is the least desirable path. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions in the VRFs. Syntax: bgp bestpath med confed.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp bestpath med confed command?
The syntax of the bgp bestpath med confed command is: bgp bestpath med confed.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp cluster-id {<IPv4-ADDR> | <ID>} command?
The syntax of the bgp cluster-id {<IPv4-ADDR> | <ID>} command is: bgp cluster-id {<IPv4-ADDR> | <ID>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp confederation <AS-NUMBER> command do?

The bgp confederation <AS-NUMBER> command configures a BGP confederation with the confederation identifier. The group of Autonomous Systems (ASs) will be presented as a single autonomous system with the confederation identifier as the AS number. The no form of the command deletes the BGP confederation identifier. Configures BGP confederation peers with both same and different sub-autonomous system to establish an eBGP membership. You can configure a list of AS numbers separated by spaces. The no form of this command disables the peer session and deletes the peer information. Syntax: bgp confederation <AS-NUMBER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp confederation <AS-NUMBER> command?
The syntax of the bgp confederation <AS-NUMBER> command is: bgp confederation <AS-NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp default local-preference <NUMBER> command do?

The bgp default local-preference <NUMBER> command default local preference value for BGP learned routes. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. The no form of this command sets the local preference to the default value of 100. Syntax: bgp default local-preference <NUMBER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp default local-preference <NUMBER> command?
The syntax of the bgp default local-preference <NUMBER> command is: bgp default local-preference <NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp deterministric-med command do?

The bgp deterministric-med command enables comparison of the Multi-Exit Discriminator (MED) attribute when selecting routes advertised by different peers in the same autonomous system. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. The no form of this command sets MED comparison to the default setting of disabled. Sets the switch to reset the BGP sessions of any directly adjacent external peers if the link used to reach them goes down. The no form of this command disables session reset. Syntax: bgp deterministric-med.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp deterministric-med command?
The syntax of the bgp deterministric-med command is: bgp deterministric-med.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp graceful-restart restart-time <DELAY> command do?

The bgp graceful-restart restart-time <DELAY> command sets the graceful restart timer which determines how long the switch waits for a graceful-restart capable neighbor to re-establish BGP peering. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. The no form of this command resets to the default value of 120 seconds. Syntax: bgp graceful-restart restart-time <DELAY>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp graceful-restart restart-time <DELAY> command?
The syntax of the bgp graceful-restart restart-time <DELAY> command is: bgp graceful-restart restart-time <DELAY>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp log-neighbor-changes command do?

The bgp log-neighbor-changes command sets the stale path timer. This timer determines how long BGP keeps stale routes from the restarting BGP peer. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. The no form of this command resets to the stale path timer to the default of 300 seconds. Enables logging of BGP neighbor session state changes. The no form of this command disables logging of changes in BGP neighbor adjacencies. Syntax: bgp log-neighbor-changes.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp log-neighbor-changes command?
The syntax of the bgp log-neighbor-changes command is: bgp log-neighbor-changes.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the bgp maxas-limit <LENGTH> command do?

The bgp maxas-limit <LENGTH> command specifies the maximum size of AS paths in update messages. Routes with AS paths greater than the specified length are discarded. The no form of this command sets the limit to the default of 32. Configures a fixed router ID for the BGP peer process running on the router. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. The no form of this command removes the fixed router ID from the running configuration and restores the default router ID selection. Syntax: bgp maxas-limit <LENGTH>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the bgp maxas-limit <LENGTH> command?
The syntax of the bgp maxas-limit <LENGTH> command is: bgp maxas-limit <LENGTH>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the clear bgp command do?

The clear bgp command resets BGP peer sessions. Sends a route refresh request when you have specified soft in. Optionally, you can specify reset for a specific VRF. Syntax: clear bgp [vrf <VRF-NAME>][IPv4 unicast | IPv6 unicast | l2vpn evpn | all].

syntax: clear bgp [vrf <VRF-NAME>][IPv4 unicast | IPv6 unicast | l2vpn evpn | all]

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the clear bgp command?
The syntax of the clear bgp command is: clear bgp [vrf <VRF-NAME>][IPv4 unicast | IPv6 unicast | l2vpn evpn | all]

syntax: clear bgp [vrf <VRF-NAME>][IPv4 unicast | IPv6 unicast | l2vpn evpn | all]

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the disable | enable command do?

The disable | enable command this command disables or enables the BGP instance while retaining the configuration. Disable and enable of the BGP instance may result in a change of the router ID. By default the BGP instance is enabled. Configures the administrative distance for BGP. The no form of this command restores the default settings, 20 for eBGP and 200 for iBGP, . Syntax: disable | enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the disable | enable command?
The syntax of the disable | enable command is: disable | enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the maximum-paths <MAXPATHS> command do?

The maximum-paths <MAXPATHS> command configures the maximum number of paths that BGP adds to the route table for equal-cost multipath (ECMP) load balancing for routes learned from both internal and external BGP. Any changes in BGP configuration are applied by restarting the current BGP sessions on the VRFs. The no form of this command restores the default setting of 4. Syntax: maximum-paths <MAXPATHS>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the maximum-paths <MAXPATHS> command?
The syntax of the maximum-paths <MAXPATHS> command is: maximum-paths <MAXPATHS>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor activate command do?

The neighbor activate command this command enables the address-family capability and exchange of information specific to an address family with a BGP neighbor. The no form of this command removes the address-family capability and disables the exchange of routes for the specified address-family with the BGP neighbor. Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDR> activate.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor activate command?
The syntax of the neighbor activate command is: neighbor <IP-ADDR> activate.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor advertisement-interval command do?

The neighbor advertisement-interval command sets the advertisement interval, which defines the length of time between transmission of BGP routing updates. The no form of this command restores the default value. Default values are 30 seconds for external BGP peer and 5 seconds for internal BGP peer. 204. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} advertisement-interval <INTERVAL>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor advertisement-interval command?
The syntax of the neighbor advertisement-interval command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} advertisement-interval <INTERVAL>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor add-paths command do?

The neighbor add-paths command enables additional path capability for BGP as described in RFC 7911. This allows BGP peer to send, receive, or send and receive multiple paths for the same address prefix without the subsequent advertisements implicitly replacing any previous paths. The additional path includes the first (N-1) best paths, which means that the total paths for an address prefix received by a BGP speaker will include the best path and the additional paths determined by its BGP peer. With additional path feature, each path is identified by a path identifier in addition to the address prefix. To use this command, the backup path of BGP next-hop must be different than the primary path. The no form of this command disables the additional path feature. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} add-paths {send | recv | both}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor add-paths command?
The syntax of the neighbor add-paths command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} add-paths {send | recv | both}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor add-paths advertise-best command?
The syntax of the neighbor add-paths advertise-best command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} add-paths advertise-best <2-4>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor allowas-in command?
The syntax of the neighbor allowas-in command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} allowas-in <LIMIT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor capability orf prefix-list command do?

The neighbor capability orf prefix-list command enables the Outbound Route filtering (ORF) capability with the neighbor in one of the three available modes. The available modes are send, receive, and both. The ORF capability is executed based on prefix list only. The Outbound Route Filtering (ORF) capability provides a mechanism for a BGP speaker to send a set of Outbound Route Filters (ORFs) that can be used by its BGP peer to filter its outbound routing updates to the speaker. This is a filtering method used to reduce the computation on the router receiving the route. The no form of this command disables the ORF capability. Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> capability orf prefix-list {send | receive | both}.

syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> capability orf prefix-list {send | receive | both}

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor capability orf prefix-list command?
The syntax of the neighbor capability orf prefix-list command is: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> capability orf prefix-list {send | receive | both}.

syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> capability orf prefix-list {send | receive | both}

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor default-originate command do?

The neighbor default-originate command enables the local router to send the default route 0.0.0.0 to a neighbor. The neighbor can then use this route to reach the router when all other routes are unavailable. Use the route-map option to configure the route map to modify the default route attributes. The no form of this command disables this feature. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} default-originate [route-map <MAP-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor default-originate command?
The syntax of the neighbor default-originate command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} default-originate [route-map <MAP-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor ebgp-multihop command?
The syntax of the neighbor ebgp-multihop command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ebgp-multihop <HOP-COUNT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor fall-over command do?

The neighbor fall-over command enables BGP fast peering session deactivation. When neighbor fall-over is configured, the BGP process monitors the RIB and if the route to peer is not present in the routing table, it immediately deactivates the peer session without waiting for the hold down timer. It is disabled by default. The no form of this command disables this feature. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor fall-over command?
The syntax of the neighbor fall-over command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} fall-over.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor graceful-shutdown command do?

The neighbor graceful-shutdown command configures the wait time before shutting down the BGP neighbor session, and can also configure the local preference value to be advertised before graceful shutdown. The no form of this command sets the wait time to the default value of 180 seconds and the local- preference value to the default of 0. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} graceful-shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor graceful-shutdown command?
The syntax of the neighbor graceful-shutdown command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} graceful-shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor local-as command do?

The neighbor local-as command configures an alternate local AS number that can be used to establish a session with a peer, allowing a router to appear to be a member of a second autonomous system (AS), and its real AS. Local AS allows two autonomous systems to merge without modifying peering arrangements. This command is valid only for external peers. The no form of this command restores the default, which is for a peering session to be established using the primary AS (primary AS is the AS number specified at the time of neighbor creation using the command neighbor remote-as). Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} local-as <AS-NUMBER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor local-as command?
The syntax of the neighbor local-as command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} local-as <AS-NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor next-hop-self command do?

The neighbor next-hop-self command configures the router as the next hop for a BGP-speaking neighbor or peer group, and enables BGP to send itself as the next hop for advertised routes. The no form of this command resets the peer nexthop-self status to default. The next hop will be generated based on the IP. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} next-hop-self.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor next-hop-self command?
The syntax of the neighbor next-hop-self command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} next-hop-self.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor next-hop-unchanged command do?

The neighbor next-hop-unchanged command enables the neighbor to preserve next-hop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. The no form of this command resets the peer next-hop-unchanged status to default. Syntax: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> next-hop-unchanged.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor next-hop-unchanged command?
The syntax of the neighbor next-hop-unchanged command is: neighbor <IP-ADDRESS> next-hop-unchanged.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor passive command do?

The neighbor passive command configures a specific neighbor, or all neighbors in a peer-group, as passive, which means that they will not initiate the TCP session. The neighbor connection must be reset using clear ip bgp for this setting to take effect. The no form of this command enables the neighbor to initiate the TCP session. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} passive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor passive command?
The syntax of the neighbor passive command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} passive.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor port command?
The syntax of the neighbor port command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} port <NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor remote-as command do?

The neighbor remote-as command creates a peer, initiates the connection to the peer, and adds an entry to the BGP neighbor table. Specifies a neighbor with an autonomous system (AS) number that identifies the neighbor as internal to the local autonomous system. Otherwise, the neighbor is considered as external. By default, neighbors that are defined using this command, exchange only unicast address prefixes. The no form of this command disables the peer session and deletes the peer information. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} remote-as <AS-NUMBER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor remote-as command?
The syntax of the neighbor remote-as command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDR>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} remote-as <AS-NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor remove-private-AS command?
The syntax of the neighbor remove-private-AS command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} remove-private-AS.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor route-map command do?
The neighbor route-map command is documented in this guide. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} route-map <MAP-NAME> {in|out}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor route-map command?
The syntax of the neighbor route-map command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} route-map <MAP-NAME> {in|out}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor route-reflector-client command do?

The neighbor route-reflector-client command this command configures the router as a BGP route reflector and the specified peer as its client. The no form of this command disables this function. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} route-reflector-client.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor route-reflector-client command?
The syntax of the neighbor route-reflector-client command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} route-reflector-client.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor send-community command do?

The neighbor send-community command this command allows community values to be sent to a specific neighbor. When this command is configured for the peer-group, then all the neighbors that are part of peer-group will send the community values to the peers. The parameters standard and extended send only the respective community numbers. When the command is issued without either of these parameters, both standard and extended communities will be sent to the neighbor. The no form of this command will not allow the neighbor to send community values to the specific neighbors that are part of peer-group. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} send-community [standard | extended].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor send-community command?
The syntax of the neighbor send-community command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} send-community [standard | extended]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor shutdown command do?

The neighbor shutdown command this command disables the peer session, terminates any active session for the specified neighbor or peer group, and removes all associated routing information. This action can cause the sudden termination of many peering sessions. The no form of this command enables the peer session for the specified neighbor. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor shutdown command?
The syntax of the neighbor shutdown command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} shutdown.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor soft-reconfiguration inbound command do?

The neighbor soft-reconfiguration inbound command this command enables you to generate inbound updates from a neighbor and change and activate BGP policies without clearing the BGP session. Changes in BGP policies require the BGP session to be cleared which can have a large negative impact on network operations. The no form of this command disables this setting. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} soft-reconfiguration inbound.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor soft-reconfiguration inbound command?
The syntax of the neighbor soft-reconfiguration inbound command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} soft-reconfiguration inbound.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor timers command do?

The neighbor timers command this command sets the timers for a specific BGP neighbor or peer group. When the timer is applied to peer- group then all the neighbors that are part of peer-group will inherit the value configured. 228. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} timers <KEEPALIVE> <HOLDTIME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor timers command?
The syntax of the neighbor timers command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} timers <KEEPALIVE> <HOLDTIME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor ttl-security-hops command do?

The neighbor ttl-security-hops command this command enables BGP to establish connection with external peers residing on networks that are not directly connected. By enabling this feature, the received TTL from a BGP peer is compared with the difference "255 - hop-count". BGP messages coming with a TTL less than this value are not accepted. BGP peering will not be established if the TTL in the session establishment is received with a lower value. Also, by enabling this feature the router will send BGP packets with TTL value of 255 to the neighbor. For a neighbor, either TTL security or ebgp-multihop can be configured, not both together. If there are multiple paths to reach the node, then the hop count should be configured considering the longest route. The no form of this command disables the peer ttl-security-hop feature. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ttl-security-hops <HOP-COUNT>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor ttl-security-hops command?
The syntax of the neighbor ttl-security-hops command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS> | <PEER-GROUP-NAME>} ttl-security-hops <HOP-COUNT>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the neighbor weight command do?

The neighbor weight command this command assigns a weight to a neighbor connection. When the weight is applied to a peer-group then all the neighbors that are part of the peer-group will inherit the value configured. The no form of this command removes a weight assignment. Syntax: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} weight <WEIGHT-VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the neighbor weight command?
The syntax of the neighbor weight command is: neighbor {<IP-ADDRESS>|<PEER-GROUP-NAME>} weight <WEIGHT-VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the network {[<IPv4/MASK> | <IPv6/MASK>]} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command do?

The network {[<IPv4/MASK> | <IPv6/MASK>]} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command this command specifies the networks to be advertised by the Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) routing processes. The no form of this command removes an entry from the routing table. Syntax: network {[<IPv4/MASK> | <IPv6/MASK>]} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the network {[<IPv4/MASK> | <IPv6/MASK>]} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command?
The syntax of the network {[<IPv4/MASK> | <IPv6/MASK>]} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>] command is: network {[<IPv4/MASK> | <IPv6/MASK>]} [route-map <ROUTE-MAP-NAME>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router bgp command do?

The router bgp command this command configures the BGP instance on the router, configures the AS (Autonomous System) the router belongs to, and enters into the BGP router configuration mode. Only a single BGP AS number can be assigned for the entire system. The no form of the command deletes the BGP instance from the router. Syntax: router bgp <AS-NUMBER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router bgp command?
The syntax of the router bgp command is: router bgp <AS-NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show bgp flap-statistics command do?
The show bgp flap-statistics command displays all the flapped and suppressed routes. 240. Syntax: show bgp {vrf <VRF_NAME> | all-vrf} [{IPv4 unicast | IPv6 unicast | all}] flap-statistics.

syntax: show bgp {vrf <VRF_NAME> | all-vrf} [{IPv4 unicast | IPv6 unicast | all}] flap-statistics

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show bgp flap-statistics command?
The syntax of the show bgp flap-statistics command is: show bgp {vrf <VRF_NAME> | all-vrf} [{IPv4 unicast | IPv6 unicast | all}] flap-statistics.

syntax: show bgp {vrf <VRF_NAME> | all-vrf} [{IPv4 unicast | IPv6 unicast | all}] flap-statistics

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the show running-config bgp command do?
The show running-config bgp command this command shows all configured BGP commands. Syntax: show running-config bgp [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show running-config bgp command?
The syntax of the show running-config bgp command is: show running-config bgp [vsx-peer]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip aspath-list command do?

The ip aspath-list command configures an AS Path list to match a specific AS path. AS Path lists are named lists of regular expression rules. They are used to match AS Path attributes in the routes for inclusion in or exclusion from route 268. Syntax: ip aspath-list <ASPATH-LIST-NAME> [seq <SEQ>] {permit | deny} <REGEXP>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip aspath-list command?
The syntax of the ip aspath-list command is: ip aspath-list <ASPATH-LIST-NAME> [seq <SEQ>] {permit | deny} <REGEXP>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip prefix-list command?
The syntax of the ip prefix-list command is: ip prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> [seq <SEQ>] {ip | IPv6} <IP-PREFIX/MASK> [ge <0-128>] [le <0-128>]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the route-map <NAME> {permit | deny} seq <NUMBER> command?
The syntax of the route-map <NAME> {permit | deny} seq <NUMBER> command is: route-map <NAME> {permit | deny} seq <NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the continue <SEQUENCE NUMBER> command do?

The continue <SEQUENCE NUMBER> command allows you to execute additional entries in a route map. The sequence number specifies the route-map entry's sequence number that will be executed next if the existing entry's match clause is successful. If a successful match occurs and continue command exists, the route map saves the set value first and then jumps to the specified route map entry. Set clauses are saved during the match clause evaluation and are executed only after the route map evaluation is completed. The set clauses are executed in the order in which they were configured. Set clauses can be accumulative or additive as set as-path prepend or it can be absolute as set metric. For set commands that configures an accumulative value, subsequent values are added in order in which they were configured. Syntax: continue <SEQUENCE NUMBER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the continue <SEQUENCE NUMBER> command?
The syntax of the continue <SEQUENCE NUMBER> command is: continue <SEQUENCE NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the match aspath-list <ASPATH-LIST-NAME> command do?

The match aspath-list <ASPATH-LIST-NAME> command matches the AS path attribute of the route with one or more regular expressions in the AS path list. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the AS path attribute of the route. Syntax: match aspath-list <ASPATH-LIST-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the match aspath-list <ASPATH-LIST-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match aspath-list <ASPATH-LIST-NAME> command is: match aspath-list <ASPATH-LIST-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the match interface <INTERFACE-NAME> command do?

The match interface <INTERFACE-NAME> command matches the community number attribute of the route with one, or more regular expressions in the community-list. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the community number attribute of the route. Matches the outgoing interface value of the route with the value configured in the match clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the outgoing interface value of the route. Syntax: match interface <INTERFACE-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the match interface <INTERFACE-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match interface <INTERFACE-NAME> command is: match interface <INTERFACE-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the match ip address prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command do?

The match ip address prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command matches the destination IP address prefix of the routes with one or more addresses in the prefix list. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the destination IP address prefix of the routes to their default value. Matches the next-hop address of the route with the configured address in the match clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the next-hop address of the route. Syntax: match ip address prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the match ip address prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match ip address prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command is: match ip address prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the match ip route-source prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command do?

The match ip route-source prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command matches the IP address of the source of the route using IP prefix lists. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the IP address of the route. Syntax: match ip route-source prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the match ip route-source prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command?
The syntax of the match ip route-source prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME> command is: match ip route-source prefix-list <PREFIX-LIST-NAME>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the match local-preference <VALUE> command do?

The match local-preference <VALUE> command matches the local preference value of the route with the value configured in the match clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the local preference value of the route. Matches the MED value of the route with the value configured in the match clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the MED value of the route. Syntax: match local-preference <VALUE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the match local-preference <VALUE> command?
The syntax of the match local-preference <VALUE> command is: match local-preference <VALUE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the match origin {igp | egp | incomplete} command do?

The match origin {igp | egp | incomplete} command matches the route origin attribute of the route with route configured in the match clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the route origin attribute of the route. Matches the metric-type value of the OSPF external route with the value configured in the match clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the metric-type value of the OSPF external route. Syntax: match origin {igp | egp | incomplete}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the match origin {igp | egp | incomplete} command?
The syntax of the match origin {igp | egp | incomplete} command is: match origin {igp | egp | incomplete}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the match source-protocol {bgp | connected | ospf | static} command do?

The match source-protocol {bgp | connected | ospf | static} command matches the source routing protocol value of the route with the value configured in the match clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not matching the source routing protocol value of the route. Syntax: match source-protocol {bgp | connected | ospf | static}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the match source-protocol {bgp | connected | ospf | static} command?
The syntax of the match source-protocol {bgp | connected | ospf | static} command is: match source-protocol {bgp | connected | ospf | static}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the set as-path exclude <AS> command do?

The set as-path exclude <AS> command matches the tag value of the route with the one configured in the match clause. Applies to static routes that will be redistributed to ospfv2 and ospfv3 protocols. The no form of this command removes the tag value of the route. Removes all occurrences of the configured AS Path from the AS Path attribute of the route. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not modifying the AS Path attribute list. Syntax: set as-path exclude <AS>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the set as-path exclude <AS> command?
The syntax of the set as-path exclude <AS> command is: set as-path exclude <AS>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the set dampening {half-life <VALUE> reuse <VALUE> suppress <VALUE> max-suppress-time <VALUE>} command do?

The set dampening {half-life <VALUE> reuse <VALUE> suppress <VALUE> max-suppress-time <VALUE>} command sets parameters of route flap dampening feature. Syntax: set dampening {half-life <VALUE> reuse <VALUE> suppress <VALUE> max-suppress-time <VALUE>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the set dampening {half-life <VALUE> reuse <VALUE> suppress <VALUE> max-suppress-time <VALUE>} command?

The syntax of the set dampening {half-life <VALUE> reuse <VALUE> suppress <VALUE> max-suppress-time <VALUE>} command is: set dampening {half-life <VALUE> reuse <VALUE> suppress <VALUE> max-suppress-time <VALUE>}.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the set {ip | IPv6} nexthop {global} <IP-ADDR> command do?

The set {ip | IPv6} nexthop {global} <IP-ADDR> command sets the IP address of the next-hop of the route with the value configured in the set clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not modifying the IP address of the next-hop of the route. Modifies the local-preference attribute of the route entry with the value configured in the set clause. The no form of this command restores the default behavior of not modifying the local-preference attribute of the route entry. Syntax: set {ip | IPv6} nexthop {global} <IP-ADDR>.

Modifies the route origin attribute of the route update with the value configured in the set clause. For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what restrictions or notes are documented for Guidelines and limitations?

For Guidelines and limitations, the guide documents the following restrictions, notes, or considerations: • VRRP must be enabled at the global level using the router vrrp enable command. It must also be enabled at the interface level at which you configure VRRP. • A maximum of 8 IPv4 and 8 IPv6 VRRP groups are supported on an interface. • A maximum of 256 VRRP groups is supported on a router. The groups can be IPv4 or IPv6. • Supported virtual router identification (VRID) range is 1-255. A maximum of 8 VRIDs can be used to achieve a scale of 256 VRRP groups by using the same VRID across different interfaces. • Proxy-ARP and Active-Gateway are not supported in conjunction with VRRP.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the address <IP-ADDR> [ primary | secondary ] command do?

The address <IP-ADDR> [ primary | secondary ] command configures a primary or secondary IPv4 or IPv6 address for the VRRP group. To use secondary IP addresses in a VRRP group, you must first configure a primary IP address on the same group. A maximum of 16 IP addresses per IPv4 VRRP group and 8 IPv6 addresses per IPv6 VRRP group are supported. NOTE: Do not configure an IPv4 VRRP group using addresses from the /30, /31, and /32 subnets of the interface IP address. NOTE: 16 Virtual IP addresses per IPv4 VR and 8 Virtual IP addresses per IPv6 VR are supported. The total number of VIP s supported by a switch is: • 1024 VIP s for IPv4 VRs • 512 VIP s for IPv6 VRs The no form of this command deletes a primary or secondary IPv4 or IPv6 address from the VRRP group. Syntax: address <IP-ADDR> [ primary | secondary ].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the address <IP-ADDR> [ primary | secondary ] command?
The syntax of the address <IP-ADDR> [ primary | secondary ] command is: address <IP-ADDR> [ primary | secondary ]
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the authentication {<text> | <md5>} {<plaintext> | <ciphertext>} <key-string> command?
The syntax of the authentication {<text> | <md5>} {<plaintext> | <ciphertext>} <key-string> command is: authentication {<text> | <md5>} {<plaintext> | <ciphertext>} <key-string>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the preempt command do?

The preempt command enables the preempt option. The default value is enabled. In default mode, a backup router with a higher priority than another backup that is operating as master will take over the master function. Applies to VRRP backup routers only and is used to minimize network disruption caused by unnecessary preemption of the master operation among backup routers. The no form of this command disables the preempt option, thus preventing the higher-priority backup from taking over the master operation from a lower-priority backup. This command does not prevent an owner router from resuming the master function after recovering from being unavailable. Syntax: preempt.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the preempt command?
The syntax of the preempt command is: preempt.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the preempt delay minimum <DELAY-IN-SECONDS> command do?

The preempt delay minimum <DELAY-IN-SECONDS> command sets the time in seconds (1-3600) that the router will wait before taking control of the virtual IP and starting to route packets. The no form of this command sets the preempt delay for the VRRP group to the default preempt delay of 0 seconds. The VRRP Preempt Delay Timer (PDT) allows admin users to configure a period of time before the VR takes control of the virtual IP address. It does not transition to the Master state until the timer period expires. The timer value configured should be long enough to allow upper layer protocol to converge. The PDT is applied only during initialization of the router. Sets the priority for the VRRP group. 302. Syntax: preempt delay minimum <DELAY-IN-SECONDS>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the preempt delay minimum <DELAY-IN-SECONDS> command?
The syntax of the preempt delay minimum <DELAY-IN-SECONDS> command is: preempt delay minimum <DELAY-IN-SECONDS>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router vrrp enable command do?
The router vrrp enable command enables VRRP protocol globally. You must globally enable the VRRP feature for VRRP virtual router. Syntax: router vrrp enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router vrrp enable command?
The syntax of the router vrrp enable command is: router vrrp enable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the router vrrp disable command do?
The router vrrp disable command disables VRRP protocol globally. Does not remove all VRRP configuration. Syntax: router vrrp disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router vrrp disable command?
The syntax of the router vrrp disable command is: router vrrp disable.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the no router vrrp command do?
The no router vrrp command removes VRRP configuration. If auto-confirm is enabled or VRRP is not configured on any interface, this command will not ask for user confirmation. Syntax: no router vrrp.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the no router vrrp command?
The syntax of the no router vrrp command is: no router vrrp.

The show track command shows all or specific track object information. Syntax: show track [brief | <OBJECT-ID>].

The syntax of the show track command is: show track [brief | <OBJECT-ID>]

The show track brief command shows brief information for all track objects. Syntax: show track brief.

The syntax of the show track brief command is: show track brief.

messages.content: For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the track OBJECT-ID command do?

The track OBJECT-ID command sets the track object ID (1-128) for the group. The track object is first configured globally for the interface and then attached to the VRRP virtual router. NOTE: The track object must not track the same interface for which a VRRP group is configured. The no form of this command removes the track object ID from the group. Syntax: track OBJECT-ID.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the track OBJECT-ID command?
The syntax of the track OBJECT-ID command is: track OBJECT-ID.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the track <OBJECT-ID> command do?

The track <OBJECT-ID> command configures a track object that can be associated with an interface. A change in interface state will then affect the priority of a VRRP group. By default, no interface is associated to a track object, so state is down. The no form of this command deletes a tracked object for an interface. If it is not associated with a VRRP virtual router, a track object cannot be deleted. NOTE: Track cannot be configured by using port with no routing. Specifies an interface to be tracked when changes in the state of the interface affect the priority of a VRRP group. Once track is associated with an interface, the track state reflects the interface forwarding state. The no form of this command removes an interface from tracking, affecting VRRP states of any interfaces associated with VRRP groups. Syntax: track <OBJECT-ID>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the track <OBJECT-ID> command?
The syntax of the track <OBJECT-ID> command is: track <OBJECT-ID>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the version <VERSION-NUMBER> command do?

The version <VERSION-NUMBER> command sets the protocol version for the VRRP group. Version change is allowed only for the IPv4 address-family. The default value is 2, which supports IPv4 with minimum 1 second advertisement interval. Value 3 supports IPv4 and IPv6 with minimum 1 second advertisement interval. Syntax: version <VERSION-NUMBER>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the version <VERSION-NUMBER> command?
The syntax of the version <VERSION-NUMBER> command is: version <VERSION-NUMBER>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what routing behavior is documented for Virtual Routing and Forwarding overview?

Virtual Routing and Forwarding: Virtual Routing and Forwarding (VRF) allows a physical router to be partitioned into multiple Virtual Router Forwarding instances (VRDs). The control and data plane are isolated in each VRF. This isolation keeps the traffic from crossing VRFs therefore multiple different routing tables can coexist within the same physical L3 switch/router. Route leaking allows for routes to “leak” or be distributed, across multiple VRFs in a controlled manner. Two methods to leak routes from one VRF to another are: • Static Routes • MP-BGP (or Dynamic Route leaking using route-targets). Static VRF route leak Static VRF route leaking between two VRFs is allowed using an IP route configuration command. To accomplish the route leak: • The routes being leaked to other VRFs must be present in the source VRF. • When a route leak has been accomplished and is therefore present in the source VRF, that route can then be leaked to other VRFs. Dynamic VRF route leak Dynamic VRF route leaking is allowed between two or more user configured VRFs.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the IPv6 route source interface command do?

The IPv6 route source interface command creates a route leak between the source VRF and destination VRF. Using the static method, the route must first be added to the destination VRF. The route is added to the local interface of the source VRF with a next- hop interface. The existing IPv6 route command takes the source interface only when next-hop IP is link- local. To support VRF route leaking for global IPv6 unicast addresses, the command takes next-hop interface information along with next-hop IP regardless of next-hop IP is link-local or not. • Users must provide both the next-hop IP and the interface information to leak the global unicast IPv6 network routes (route that is not directly reachable). • The next-hop IP information is not required to leak connected global unicast IPv6 routes (route that is directly reachable). Syntax: IPv6 route <IPv6 ADDR/MEMBER><IPv6 ADDR> source <INTERFACE>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the IPv6 route source interface command?
The syntax of the IPv6 route source interface command is: IPv6 route <IPv6 ADDR/MEMBER><IPv6 ADDR> source <INTERFACE>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the ip route interface command do?

The ip route interface command creates a route leak between the SRC-VRF and DST-VRF. Using the static method, the route must first be added to the destination VRF. The route is added to the local interface of the source VRF with a next-hop interface. The existing IP route command can then take either the next-hop IP or the next-hop interface. 322. Syntax: ip route <IPv4 ADDR/MEMBER> interface <IPv4 ADDR>.
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the ip route interface command?
The syntax of the ip route interface command is: ip route <IPv4 ADDR/MEMBER> interface <IPv4 ADDR>
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what does the route-target [import | export | both] <AS-NUMBER: NN> command do?

The route-target [import | export | both] <AS-NUMBER: NN> command bGP route targets are extended BGP communities that identify the VPNv4 or VPNv6 routes that are associated with a VRF. This command specifies the route targets used on the import or export of the routes to other VRFs. Multiple route targets can be associated to a VRF. The no form of the command removes the association. Syntax: route-target [import | export | both] <AS-NUMBER: NN>.

The syntax of the route-target [import | export | both] <AS-NUMBER: NN> command is: route-target [import | export | both] <AS-NUMBER: NN>

The router bgp vrf command is documented in this guide. Syntax: router bgp <AS-NUMBER: NN> [vrf <VRF-NAME>].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the router bgp vrf command?
The syntax of the router bgp vrf command is: router bgp <AS-NUMBER: NN> [vrf <VRF-NAME>]

For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show bgp info vrf command?
The syntax of the show bgp info vrf command is: show bgp info vrf <vrf-name> [vsx-peer]

The show ip route vrf command shows route information for specified VRF. Syntax: show ip route vrf <vrf-name> [vsx-peer].
For 8320 AOS-CX 10.06, what is the syntax of the show ip route vrf command?
The syntax of the show ip route vrf command is: show ip route vrf <vrf-name> [vsx-peer]
